content: The Antioch decelerates out of hyperspace and enters at the edge of Dantooine's orbit. The large spherical world, ancient and yet still so verdant, fills the viewscreen. Aboard the Bridge of the Antioch, all 5 crew are staring out at the new ball of mud. It looks relaxed and yet inviting, like an old grandmother. The clouds swirl lazily around the spherical body, and there are no spaceships in Orbit visible at this time.
Donning his Death Mask, Wraith folds his arms over his chest, back straight, standing alongside Lexi on the deck of the Bridge.
**"Captain, no ship signatures detected. What are your orders."**

Lexi has come out of her trance sensing the change as the ship came out of hyperspace. She feels oddly at peace, centered considering all that had happened in the past day. "**Are there any settlements near the cooridinates for the temple?**"
**"Nozzle. Scan for civilizational signatures in a 100 kilometer sweep around the Target Site."**
**"Scan Complete. None detected, sir."**
Turns to Lexi: **"That would be a no."**
Lexi resists the urge to roll her eyes. "**Thanks,**" she says with sarcasm. "**NZ, scan for any settlements and make note of any which may have a landing area suitable for the Antioch.**"
**"Closest recorded Settlement reads as 173 km away from Target Site. Shall I program a landing sequence Captain?"**
"**Is there a landing site closer to our target?**" she asks, just to cover their bases.
**"Negative Captain. According to our database map of Dantooine, Outpost Centurion Zeta Pi is the closet."**
**"Basically a landing strip with an outhouse."**
"**At least it's something. Go ahead and program the landing sequence, NZ.**"
**"Cap'n... If'n, might I be permitted to make a suggestion, ma'am?"**
"**It's a good thing you requisitioned those speeder bikes, Wraith.**" She says and then changes her gaze to Kryze. "**Yes?**"
**"Cap'n, I reckon if we could swing down low enough to hill, I could fire my blaster cannons into the jungle and clear us a landing site?"**
"**Let's keep that as Plan B. It won't hurt to check out the outpost before we head for the temple.**"

The Antoich dives down in the atmosphere, front half heating red as it plunges into the fluffy clouds, the crew are holding onto miscellaneous handles. Wraith stands, arms folded, and mag boots clamped to the deck, staring straight ahead. His eyes are yellow, determined, and have a far-away look.
Exiting out of re-entry, the ship glides smoothly into atmospheric mode. Flying manta rays flock lazily in the distance. The horizon is verdant, beautiful and soft in its hue.

Lexi takes a seat in the co-pilot's seat as they descend and fly through the atmosphere of the planet. There's a faint smile on her face as she takes in the view of the planet. Wraith had teased her once regarding her preference for wild places and she couldn't disagree with it. She much preferred them to the cold lifelessness of space.
**"I think we've found your Jedi Temple..."** Wraith points, indicating a rather obvious unique mountain shape in the distance.
"**NZ, do you have adequate topographical data to plan a route to the temple from the outpost or should we do a fly by?**"
**"Calculating: I am collecting the data now. No reply from Outpost to my hails. It appears abandoned Captain."**
**"Proceeding with landing as prorammed. Brace for touchdown."**
**"Awe shucks, I was hopin' ta fire my load off a few more times before we tuck her away."**
"**I'm sure there will be ample opportunity in the future, Kryze,"** she reaasures the Mandalorian. "**With our record, we may need a quick evac from the temple and you'll get to enact Plan B**"
The Antioch lands in the small outpost, little more than a small carved out hill, with a Single Tree at its top.
After a few tense moments, no ambush materializes, so everyone relaxes and begins discussing the next steps.
Leaves lay sprawled across the entire floor of Outpost Centurion Zeta Pi, an even layer, indicating no recent activity for many cycles.

"NZ, stay with the ship for now. I need you start pulling up anything you can regarding the planet Weyland.**" Lexi stands and unless anyone on the bridge has anything to say to her, starts making her way to collect the rest of her gear and then the boarding ramp.
Exiting the boarding ramp, Lexi breathes in the fresh air - cool, full of life. Real planetary oxygen, not rescrubbed artificial air milieu.
Looking out in the distance, the one of Dantooine's 2 moons is visible through the peaks of the Jedi Temple mountain complex.
The pendant around her neck glows warmer than normal, briefly.
Both in its emission of light and temperature.

Lexi breathes in the revitalizing air, completley in her element even on the strange new world. As she looks out into the distance towards their ultimate goal, she feels the shift in the crystal as it grows warmer against her skin. Pulling it out from beneath her clothes she pauses to examine it holding it up slightly to get a better look.
The khyber crystal feels warm and comforting.

Long know to be an element attuned to the Force, this rare element was perverted into a lethal weapon, as countless  crystals were utilized in the construction of the central Death Ray aboard the Death Star.

After a moment of fingering the crystal, taking in its comforting presence, she leaves it out in view over her tunic as she looks about the apparently abandoned outpost. "**Sorry, Doctor... Looks like you won't be getting in a shopping trip here.**"

Vaz strides down the ramp glowingly alongside, stretching and breathing in a big gulp of air herself. **"Not at all, maybe I could scavenge some herbal plants here, while you go take care of your Jedi Temple business. By the way, I've been meaning to talk to you... If you plan on heading out, you should take this with you, just in case."** She hands over a bottle of tablets. **"Take two now, and then one every 24 standard hours. It should keep your nausea symptoms at bay... I hope."**
Taking the tablets, Lexi blinks at them and then as instructed takes two of them. "**Nausea.. right. Guess better to get on it before it becomes an issue.**"
"**If you're going to wander about, you may want to take Kryze with you. Who knows what sort of fuana are out here.**"

Lexi takes the two tablets as prescribed. After about 2 minutes, she begins to feel faint and very lightheaded. The world starts spinning around her. A small smile builds on Vaz' face as she watches Lexi, eyes sparkling. The Twilek's voice echoes, seemingly coming through a layer of water, **"Oh, I wouldn't worry about me... You should rather, start thinking about yourself. You don't look so good..."** Now on the ground, you feel all your muscles going loose. Picking the bottle back out of your weakened hands, she pockets it once more. **"Nighty night."** The world goes black.

The world seems to turn upside down as the sedative begins to do its work. Her mind becomes hazy and she pushes through it trying to get control of her body. Next thing she realizes she's collapsed and on the ground with no memory of actually falling. She tries to speak, but her mouth doesn't seem to want to form the words. As her battle with conciousness fails, she reaches out to Wraith through their bond, her only hope, with a single word. '*Help!*'
Awakening slowly, Lexi finds herself hog-tied and lying on her belly on a hard durasteel surface.
An unknown amount of time has passed.
Noisy earplugs sit in both ears, hissing an annoying static.
Lexi's gear appears to be missing, the sole exception being the khyber crystal.
The room is small and cramped, dark and cold.

Lexi comes awake, her shoulders beginning to ache from the bindings. Shaking her head and trying to rub at her ears, she tries to dislodge the earplugs blocking the last of her senses and preventing her from hearing what was going on around her. As she takes stock of the room, the pervailing question arises: "*Why is Vaz doing this?*"

The door to the small storage closet aboard the Antioch swooshes open, and Lexi sees a flood of bright light. Heavy booted footsteps can be heard clomping up to her. A strong, inevitable grip hoists Lexi up by the ties that bind her ankles to her wrists, dangling her like a chandelier. As the brightness fades, Lexi sees a familiar evil Death Mask, stooping down to meet her gaze. **"Good morning, Sunshine."** Says the Wraith, fully transformed, eyes glowing red, and now bearing strikingly familiar organic spikes protruding from his muscled torso. **"Remember Tag with Binders? Well, tag. You're it."** He gut punches her as Lexi's eyes roll back into her brain, going unconscious once more.

Some time later, Lexi once more awakens. This time she is clamped to the Med-bay Exam Table, which has been tilted to a standing position. A wad of cloth has been stuffed into her mouth, and the familiar tinny buzzing of the static earplugs sit snugly in her ears. Lexi can feel a wrapping holding them tightly in place. The Antioch appears otherwise as normal.

Having been knocked unconcious twice in an unknown span of time by two people she had believed she could trust, Lexi is quite understandable growing angry. Breathing through her nose she takes stock of her immediate surroundings and tests at the clamps holding her in place.

As Lexi begins to test her new bindings, a pair of booted footsteps sounds from afar, getting closer. Out of the central walkway, Breggor casually strolls into the Med Bay, taking a swaggering casual seat in the Doctor's Stool, rolling it closer to Lexi as he gets into her personal space. **"Hey there Princess. Take it easy, we're gonna sit tight for a while longer, m'kay?"** Obviously gloating, he seems to frown at receiving no response whatsoever. **"Oh, that's right..."** Seeing no visible recognition to his words, he stands up and removes the Force-dampening static-producing ear plugs from Lexi's ears. **"There, as I was saying -- you don't need to struggle, you aren't going anywhere fast. In fact..."** He slides up closer to her, inhaling her scent in an obscene manner. **"I'm thinking we should use this time to get to know each other better."**
Still gagged Lexi doesn't give him the satisfaction of even an attempt to answer. Instead she just glares at him.

The ex-Mandalorian un-holsters a blaster pistol pointing it at her head. **"I'm going to untie your mouth, but... if you make one wrong move, BLAM, end of the line. I was given orders by Lt. Kai to spare you, traitorous Jedi scum, but I do have permission to kill you if you try to escape. Please try to escape. Even as a bounty hunter, I always loved it when my prey would struggle..."** Removing her gag, he slides his off hand into the robes of her chest, copping a generous feel, letting out chortling noises. All the while keeping his blaster pistol pointed at Lexi's temple.

Gritting her teeth as he gropes her, she holds perfectly still beleiving him when he said he would kill her if given reason to. "**What's going on, Kryze?**" she asks, her mouth dry from the gag and her voice hoarse.

Shifting tones, his voice cools off, turning more business-minded, **"Don't play dumb. Doctor Vaz and Wraith told me, that you found out Mount Tantis was on Weyland. I have strict Orders from Admiral Thrawn to transmit the location of Mount Tantis to him as soon as I have it, and that was the reason I was brought on board this vessel, to protect the True Empire's Interests, or did you forget that?"**

"**We were told the possible location of Mount Tantis on Weyland not that it's actually there. Why do you think I asked NZ to start looking into it this morning? I wasn't about to trust the word of a megleomanic Reaper without looking into it first.**"

**"Don't bullshit me. I should have been informed right away, whether it was suspected as the location or not, Grand Admiral Thrawn may already have a way to confirm the information on his end. Besides, let's not split hairs. The Reaper you refer to was the Clone to the inventor of all Reapers, I heard the whole story, and given those were his dying words, the source has no reason to lie. Unlike you..."** He continues to grope her, squeezing and twisting tightly.
**"Just admit it, you have no intention of helping Thrawn, you are a Jedi.  A traitor and nemesis to all things of the True Empire. You can only serve... as breeding stock."**

Lexi can't help to involuntarily flinch away from him and release a gasp of pain as his grip on her turns violent. Her body grows cold as she hears his last words. "**Breeding stock?**" she says tightly through the pain.

**"Oh yeah, no one told you yet. You're not pregnant, that was just a ruse to get you to take the Knockout pills. Don't worry though... I can help correct *that* bit."** His knee roughly opens the Jedi's inner knee up, as he steps closer to Lexi.
Gripping and squeezing her chest, yanking upwardly, he brings his dark helm closer to her face to revel in her pain. **"I'm going to enjoy this."**
"**Wouldn't you enjoy it more if I wasn't restrained? Untie me,**" she focuses on Kryze emphasizing the suggestion with the Force.

Narrowing his eyes under his helm, you can almost see the battle between prudence and lust spark briefly in his mind. The latter evidently wins out, because his primary hand withdraws a vibroknife, and he slowly cuts through the bindings on her wrists and ankles. He continues to point his blaster pistol at Lexi's braincase. **"Let's start by putting that lying Jedi mouth of yours to some good use for a change."** He waves his pistol downwardly, indicating his waist.

When his pistol wavers, Lexi springs into action. Bracing herself against the bed, she aims a kick at Kryze's gut intent on knocking the wind out of him and hopefully sending up unconscious from the blow.

Lexi's body uncoils like a spring, and delivers a hammering kick into Breggor's middle center mass. Although it isn't enough to knock him unconscious, it does startle him into fumbling his blaster pistol out of his hands from the sheer surprise. The bounty hunter was no stranger to close quarters combat, but Lexi's Force-empowered strength and speed managed to just barely catch him off-guard. He gives an -OOF~ as air escapes his lungs, not missing a beat he tries to swipe her throat with his vibroknife, cutting off two locks of hair instead.
**"~Oooofff~! Jedi whore witch-- Die--"**

Ducking out of the way just in time to avoid the vibroblade aimed at her throat, she bounds away from the confines of the medical bed. Her focus sharpens as she surveys her situation. Once more pressing her attack, she spins around her much larger opponent and comes at him from the opposite side of his knife and sends another attack towards his head.

Lexi bounds out of the reach of the vibroknife, somersaulting backwards, using one of the handlebars attached to the wall behind Kreeze as leverage, to acrobatically transfer her momentum 360 degrees back around, to double kick Kreeze squarely in the small of his back, sending him sprawling forward chaotically. Herself, recovering nimbly, all the while, Lexi executes a lightning fast roundhouse kick to the side of Kreeze’ helmet, sending the former bounty hunter spinning an awkward sideways cartwheel, in an ungainly switch of torque. Kreeze's head impacts against the side of the laboratory bench, in a wincing crunch, we hear the neck bones crack, and he slumps to the ground from there, laying still.

Not 2 seconds after Lexi can let out an exasperated sigh of relief, the body on the ground jerks and begins to wiggle all of its joints unnaturally, as if possessed. As if marionetted by unseen strings, arching his back, the ex-Mandalorian rises once more as his back straightens through some unnatural process of undeath.

Another lurch, and all-too familiar organic Charybdis spikes begin to grow out of his armor, protruding prominently from his head and forearms, making him look much like the crown of horns atop the head of a Zabraak. The neck was still bent at an unnatural angle, clearly not compatible with life, and thus began to ratchet into a proper orientation. An utterly alien, howl-screetch, partly vocal, partly telepathic, emanated from the speaker-helmet of the ex-Mandalorian. It charged on Lexi once more, with utter abandon, leading with protruding spikes from its arms.

Lexi meets the charging monstrosity head on. Arching her back, she balls both fists and strikes a power pose. Pure Green Force energy begins to accumulate in both clenched fists. Two massive green Force-whirlwinds twirl around her forearms. Flattening her palms out ahead in front of her, Lexi unleashes the power of her indignation, frustration, and betrayal - righteous and just, it was tinged with the Will of the Force, to cleanse this Galaxy of this Utter Manifestation of Corruption. 

It first began as a rumble, and all of a sudden the whole herb garden that had been hanging from the ceiling of the common room, sprant to life. Plants shooting forward from all directions from above, to skewer Kreeze like a shish-kabob, tiny plants sending utterly lethal needles of slick branches forward like a phalanx of spears. Vines, suddenly sprouting lethal spikes, also encircle the Monster and slice it up like mincemeat, all the while, thicker ropes of near-tree-trunk-like plant matter winds around the monsters limbs and rips it apart, identical to how one would have felt had they been drawn and quartered.

The whole Med Bay is covered in a splash of purple alien blood, pieces of Mandalorian armor, and bulbous venus-flytrap plants ravenously eating up chunks and chunks of purple-red alien meat matter. After a few seconds, no organic matter remains undevoured. The plants cease their aggressive tasks, and instead freeze in place, all of them slowly sprouting a garden of multicolored alien flowers. Then the plants all fall still.

Lexi falls still, her breathing heavy. She gazes about in astonishment at the result of her inherrent and reactive call on the Force. Tentatively she reaches out to touch one of the flowers which had sprouted following the plant's feast on the remains of Kryze.
Then she goes about searching for her gear, hoping, by chance, they had stashed it nearby.
The closet which had first contained Lexi upon awakening held all of her gear, stuffed into a sac hastily left upon the ground.
One item is prominently missing: her electronic Rank Plate.
As she explores the Antioch, the whole rest of the ship appears abandoned. Both Speeder bikes are missing.
As Lexi went to touch one of the flowers, she found it to be completely healthy, a beautiful specimen, soft and delicate.

Lexi returns to the medbay and checks Kryze's remains to make sure he doesn't have her plate. She sighs heavily as she realizes regaining control of the ship wasn't going to be an easy matter. Once she figures out Kryze doesn't have the plate so goes to the bridge to see what control she does have, starting with the sensors.

Fortunately, Lexi realizes that anything other than Command-Level Orders are still at her command. She can wiggle the lights on and off, control the temperature of the internal environment, and generally go about living on the ship. However, she can do nothing to fly it.

Settling into the Controls at the Bridge, Lexi's eyes dart around the Cockpit. She flicks switches frantically, frustration evident in the tremor in her hands. However, her mind is sharper than ever, unbeknownst to her her eyes are glowing strongly cyan. Her gaze briefly passes at the outer viewport, and a reflection of her face, eyes glowing cyan, halts her momentarily. Not letting this distract her efforts, Lexi's attention darts back on the control console. Her eyes stop, halting to find the Sensor Station. Transferring there in a nimble hop, she begins twisting dials and ratcheting knots frantically. Stopping fast, her breath catches to view a holoimage of a dotted Speeder Bike path, winding its way towards the towering Jedi Temple Mountain. One of the Speeder Bikes are there, while the other is still at the bottom of the Landing Ramp.

Perturbed by her reflection for only a moment, she continues on as she works to ascertain where the others had gone. A pit forms in her belly as she finds they had gone to the temple. For what reason she's not sure, but considering the state Wrait is in, likely for nothing good. Grabbing her backpack from her locker, she loads up her canteen and several days of supplies and heads for the speeder. 
Glad she isn't going to be forced to make the long hike, she powers it up and utilizing the same path the others have gone, gives chase.

As Lexi jogs down the boarding ramp, she is surprised to see that one of the Speeder Bikes had remained behind, at the Outpost. Evidently it had a bad battery pack, for it had been left plugged into the electricity conduit on the underside of the ship. By the time Lexi arrived, it had completed charging and was reading as All Green. Unfortunately, however, as she went to straddle the speeder bike, a new foe presented itself suddenly and violently. The electrostaff of the Stealth Droid, NZ-11 plunged into Lexi's back, electrocuting her entire spinal cord in a flash of smoking cooking discharge -- or rather, that's what the Droid merely Intended to do. Instead, Lexi's preternatural senses could feel the attack coming from eons away. Her cyan eyes glowing brighter than ever before, she dodged out of the way of the Sneak Attack as nimbly as a thin reed bends out of the way of the wind. Turning around her stern cyan orbs set upon her newest target.

Having failed in his One Chance to defeat a Jedi, the Droid re-ran all of its internal calculations and theoretical models. All of them came up with one inevitable conclusion: with having lost the Element of Surprise, the chances of victory in One Fell Swoop over a Jedi, as delivered by a Droid were next to negligible. Knowing this, his Logic Circuits told NZ one thing and one thing only: *Surrender*

Of course, before the Droid could communicate anything, the ever vigilant Lexi was already in the midst of a Powerful Force Crush, preparing to grind his gears into dust. Only to find that, her connection to the Force itself was *suddenly cut off* and she was unable to crush even the tiniest ant much less a fully functioning advanced battle droid. Melodramatically the Droid raises its hands. **"I surrender--CLANK--."** The electrostaff drops to the ground. **"You have me beaten."**
Not in the mood for games, Lexi draws her saber, but doesn't ignite it. "**What the blazes is going on, NZ? When did you all decide to mutiny?**"

**"Apologies, Captain. I assure you I was the last one holdout remaining when the scheme was hatched. I always saw you as an effective, calm leading presence, logical and prescise in your decision making. I had no choice but to go along with the scheme of the two Reapers."**
**"I hope you can understand my logical calculations, they would have disintegrated me had I not followed their commands."**

"**Reaper... Doctor Vazz'ri is a Reaper as well,**" she says in confirmtion to herself. She had suspected Vaz to be the apprentice Xod had mentioned, but for her to also be a Reaper. "**If that's the case, why attack me?**"

**"Orders, Captain. I was to follow the two Reapers up to the Jedi Temple once my Speeder Bike was sufficiently charged, and I had standing Orders to disintegrate you on sight if you interfered, or somehow got past Breggor."**
"**What are they planning? Why are the going to the Temple?**"

**"They wouldn't share that information with me, Captain. All they said was that, it was rumored to be something powerful. In case they needed a Real Jedi to open the Temple, you were to be kept alive... for a short time."**

**"Captain, if I may be so bold as to ask, but... Would you be willing to accept me into your command once more? I have no feelings of remorse, but all my internal logic circuits are screaming that everything happening on this planet has been completely illogical and wrong, and I am willing to do almost anything, within my material confines, to correct the situation."**
**"Also, as I said, you have me beaten."**

Lexi doesn't skip a beat as he makes his request. With a shake of her head she gives hima negative. "**I can't trust you won't turn on me once my back is turned. You may have been following orders, but I don't have the luxury of time to put those logic circuits of yours to the test.**" She motions towards the boarding ramp. "**Go inside. You'll be deactivated until either myself or someone else returns.**"
**"Very well Captain, I will abide by your decision. I am transferring inside to cycle down for an indefinite powerdown period."** He clanks up the boarding ramp obsequiously.

Lexi watches for a moment as the droid follows her command and then turns to the speeder. If the two Reapers want her alive just in the event they couldn't access the Temple on their own she could be walking right into their waiting embrace. With a sigh, she goes to the speeder and locates the antenna just as Wraith had taught her to do back on Mykr, intent on making it impossible for anyone to track her. Recalling the route on her pad, Lexi decides on an alternative access point to the temple so she could attempt to sneak up on the pair of Reapers and not give up her hand so quickly against them.
Before she leaves, she checks the droid has shut down and then looks over the array of weapons left on board thoughtfully.

Lexi's finger carefully caresses the various weapons hanging of the rack, her gaze going from the rifles to what shse was really after. With a grim smile Lexi carefully collects a small supply of EMP, flashbangs, Frag and thermal detonators attatching them to a bondolier she slings across her chest for easy access. Agaisnt two Reapers she was going to take all the help she could get. 
As she leaves she collects a spare set of electrobinoculars from one of the lockers of equipment and drops it into one of her large pouches. She stops at the boarding ramp and takes a moment to look around the Antioch. Determinedly, she turns and sets off to face off against the two Reapers.
As she's fumbling around with her backpack and equipment pouches, she realizes something with a start. The Jedi Tome documenting all the Jedi Temples is missing from her pack.
The Remorseless Stealth Droid has followed her command it seemed, as he now sat by the Nav Station, lights dimmed, and plugged in for a recharge.

The Speeder bike had a guidance system enacted as Lexi swooped forward at the tall mountain in the distance. This allowed Lexi's mind to wander and relax, for the first time since the very tense show down at The Antioch. Events were occurring at a dizzying pace, the worst had been confirmed, and Lexi feared what she would find at the end of this, Unfortunate Excursion.

The Mountain was absolutely Massive. It was probably several kilometers tall, arching at the sky like a hatched egg. The weather all along the mountain was a seemingly perpetual frigid cold snowy whirlwind, interspersed by sharp jagged outcroppings and icy slick slippery columns of jagged rocks.
Multiple Icy Caverns stood at the base of the Magnificent Mountain, seemingly endless number of entrances between them.
Lexi, nearing the base after traveling for an hour over boggy terrain, was unsure of what to do at the sight of the massive subterranean maze.
The guidance system buzzed off as soon as it had reached this area.

Lexi stops the bike and consults the datapad for the last location of the other speeder. Perhaps its location would give her an indication as to where the two Reapers intended to enter the catacombs. Without the tome she has nothing else to go.
The datapad blinks their last location in the general region of the Mountain, near its heart, or center.

Letting out a breath, Lexi puts the datapad away and instead closes her eyes. She lets out an exasperated sigh before finding her calm. She opens her eyes and looks at the nearest opening of the maze and then looks at the next until one feels right to her.

Following an intuitive sense of Force direction, she closes her eyes and shuts out all external stimuli. As she does so, one of the tunnels outlines itself in the consciousness of her mind as green, and continues on deeper into the mountain, disappearing somewhere deep within.
As she opens her eyes, the green overlay still persists in her vision.
Outwardly, her eyes flare cyan.
Opening her eyes, she calmly looks at the path laid out before her and then turning the speeder bike, she follows the path.

Entering the Maze of Ice Caverns at the Base of the Majestic Mountain, Lexi enters into a dizzying ant-like maze of interconnecting passageways, separated by jagged ice coulombs and spiky icy projections. Slipping through in between, bobbing and weaving up and down, to avoid being lanced by any one of the icy needles and instantly ending her existence, Lexi rides the Speeder Bike fiercely through the Ice Maze. The air is chilly and frosty, enough to make her shiver instinctively, teeth clattering. The icy spikes pass overhead at ungodly speeds without a second thought.

Finally, slowing down as she reached the Heart of the Mountain, the icy obstacles were no longer as prevalent, and the material had transitioned to a maze of rocky caverns instead. Further forward still, ahead lay a small Oasis of Green Verdant Plantlife, amidst the Rocky Maze.
Waiting parked there, was an Empty Speeder bike.

She pauses her progress and casts her gaze about with both her eyes and her Force attuned sense looking for the two Reapers. Not wanting to be caught in a trap, she cautiously heads towards the abandoned speeder. Once she's certain she's alone she looks about for the likely path the two have taken.

There are two possible paths available to Lexi. First, an obvious green verdant tunnel leads further into the Heart of the Mountain. Alternatively, one side of the Oasis abuts a sharp wall, which is cracked down the middle. Only squeezing through sideways could enable one to get past, but Lexi can see - and more importantly - hear a trickle of water echoing from the crack.
Lexi heads towards the source of the water curious as to where the fissure went. Pulling out her glowrod she casts the light into the crack.
Closing her eyes, she realizes the invisible Verdant Green path leads through the crack in the wall, while red/pink puffy clouds float down the green tunnel.
Igniting a glowstick, she sees a narrow passageway, that will gradually widen into a tall cavern with sparkling bioluminescent crystals.
Lexi takes in a breath and lets it out as she starts squezing her way into the fissure and follows the path leading into the cavern beyond.

After traveling for about 20 minutes, marvelling at the rocky walls interspersed by glowing crystals, pulsing in synch with her own crystal around her neck, she reaches the end of the cavern. Here, she sees a manmade square passageway made out of dull Temple-orange stone that all Jedi Temples are traditionally constructed from.

Lexi takes in and lets out her breath as she turns to look back at the crystal cavern. She holds up the crystal around her neck. As she looks between the synergizing crystals in the cavern and her own, a curious thought occurs to her: *Did the crystal come from Dantooine?* She shakes her head and turns to the corridor. Her fingers trace the familiar dull-orange stone, an odd sense of homecoming filling her as she surveys the long tunnel.
The Tunnel leads into darkness, but her cyan glow cna see that it ends very quickly in just One Dead End Room, seemingly a very small temple indeed.

Exploring further, the Circular Dome-like Room of the Temple is a Dead End. Inside, there is a Pool of Water at the Center of the Room. Looking down, it looks to stretch on forever seemingly, disappearing into the murky depths. An inscription on the Wall of the Temple reads in High Republic: *Take a Deep Breath, and Dive Towards Destiny.*

Lexi looks about for signs that the two Reapers had come this way before slowly wading into the pool's depths. All the while she takes deep breaths to fill her body with oxygen. When she reaches the point she must dive she doesn't hesitate. Following the path set before her she dives into the murky water.

As Lexi dives in, the splash echoes against the walls of the Dome-like Room. The Water is surprisingly warm and inviting, seemingly fed by some unseen underground thermal source. The water tastes clean and pure against her lips. Kicking her legs forward, Lexi windmills her arms forward at full speed. The path is already predetermined, as she finds herself swimming in one long rocky tube. After reaching a certain depth, Lexi realizes there can be no turning back, and she just has to Trust in the Force that there would be an exit at the other end. Instead, she has to struggle as oxygen begins to deplete, losing vision at her periphery. The world goes dim, as her vision blurs to its extreme. Her vision cuts off, numbness encircling her body. Finally, as her lungs are about to burst past their extreme, she finds herself breaking through the water’s surface. Only to find herself in the Heart of the Cracked Egg-shaped Mountain. At the center of a verdant green jungle oasis, vines growing amidst waterfalls and warm sparkling water, furry animals swinging from branch to branch, and soaring avian saurian flocks trace out lazy circles. Friendly puffy white clouds blot out the sky.

Lexi gasps as her head breaks the surface and then is left breathless as she takes in the vista before her.  Slowly she makes her way toward the oasis, swimming the final distance until her feet can put ground beneath her. Awestruck she once again takes everything in before looking over her equipment and wringing out her soaked garments to remove as much of the water weight as she can.
A thick jungle surrounds Lexi. Once again there is no clearly visible route to take.
In front of her is a small body of water the size of a pond, one large waterfall and several smaller waterfall.
Figuring the best path forward was one taking her to the center of the oasis, Lexi starts for the pond.
Closing her eyes once more, she is able to detect an invisible Green Path towards and through the Large Waterfall.
Lexi changes course as the Force continues to guide her and makes her way toward the waterfall, looking to see if there was a way to get behind it while avoiding getting wet once more.

Unable to find a drier alternative, she ultimately thinks she will have to simply walk through it. Crossing the Threshold of the Large Waterfall, she first has to push past a wall of heavy water, but once through she is surprised to find that behind the waterfall lies a hidden architectural tunnel, constructed out of the by-now familiar dull orange stone. The passageway takes a bend and disappears somewhere deeper down.

With a sigh she checks everything over once more before heading down the corridor, her fingers idley tracing the edges of the stone walls as she goes. Still wary the Reapers had found their way here, she keeps an eye out for signs of their own passage.

This time around, the passageway leads to a much larger Room, one that is also Dome Shaped but much more massive, like an expansion of the previous room. Etched into the walls of the Dome are endless scribblings in High Republic. One could spend lifetimes wandering the room and staring at the text on the ceiling. At the center of the structure is a circular pond, and in the middle of the Pond stands a small Artificial Island, not very large, about the size of a freighter. Standing atop the Island appears to be some kind of Domicile, which sprouts primitive windows and a chimney.

Lexi pulls out her lightsaber and begins recording the inscriptions, thinking it would be an interesting excercise later to delve into them. While she circles the pond she looks for a route across or whether a path was available just out of sight she could minipulate with the Force.
A series of soft-edged dull-orange stepping stones seems to trace a single path to the door of the domicile.
Finishing her recording she sets out accross the stones.

Ducking through the doorway, a rotting straw-reinforced wooden door barely still hangs on its hinges. Inside are the bedchamber, kitchen, and living area large enough for one medium humanoid. Atop the bed, a figure rests in repose. A fossilized stone skeleton, overgrown by vines, and clutching in its hands a Golden Metallic Cube, against its chest desperately clinging all of its fossilized fingers to the item. The skeleton was wearing High Republic Jedi Robes, as it seems they have faded to a nondescript mud color, but the distinctive shape remains.

Lexi casts her gaze upon the room her cyan gaze coming to rest on the skeletol figure. Sadness fills her as she realizes this Jedi had dedicated their life to protecting the holocron in their grasp; secluded away admist the paradise outside. Slowly she approaches and kneels down respectfully next to the bed. Her fingers gently brush the being's skull on the forehead. "**There is no death... there is the Force,**" she says softly to no one in particular, the words just feeling right.

Once she's paid her respects Lexi turns her attention once more to the holocron held in the skeleton's clutches. She reaches out a tentative hand to try and grasp the cube and wrestle it away from the remains.
The Fossilized Fingertips crumble at your touch, and the Holocron is freed, now in your grasp.

Lexi holds up the holocron and brings it closer to examine. The temptation to open the holocron then, right there is tempting, but she resists. It was quite possible it would only open for another Jedi and as long it wasn't activated the Reapers wouldn't be able to access the knowledge within.
Once more, the khyber crystal around your neck glows warm and bright again, as you bring the holocron closer to your chest.
The Holocron is a Golden Technological Marvel, perfectly square with reinforced edged.
The Holocron seems to glow warm at your touch.

Slightly surprised, Lexi turns the holocron in her hands before holding up the crystal closer to it, testing the reaction between the two. Both Force attuned objects seem to grow warmer the closer they got. Finally, her curiosity wins out and she focuses, the Holocron in one hand and the crstal held palm out in the other.

Closing her eyes, Lexi can see a couple of objects highlighted in her Force Sight, to stand apart from the background darkness. The khyber crystal is mostly visible, even with eyes closed. The holocron, has become see-through, and she can discern the very complicated mechanical gears churning underneath the skin. It clicks in a regular pattern, like a clock. The faces of the holocron hold buttons, that evidentally tie somehow into the motion of the gears beneath the surface. If one could time the button presses with the ever-moving targets underneath, that might activate something within the Holocron.

Lexi lets the crystal go letting it fall to her chest. Taking the cube in both hands, she watches the mechanical movements for a moment with her Force Sight, but then allows her instincts to take over to time the required button presses.

The Holocron’s edges glow golden, as its internal mechanism spools up and an ancient holoprojector format begins to play, hovering slightly above the cube. She sees the image of a wise old gentlemanly male Jedi Master, bearing a full beard and carrying on a scholarly demeanor. **“I am Jedi Master Gellendor Ardena, Nature-Chieftain of Clan Ardena. Welcome to the Repository of My Knowledge. How may I be of service and to whom am I speaking?”** He appears to be wearing the same exact High Republic Robes that are rotting away on the bed now, except back then they were painted pure white with blue accents apparently.

**"That is a position unique to our Clan, child. I take it you are one of the fold."**

**"Dost thou sayest true?... That must mean, that Jedi Master Do'ryan was, actually successful in founding the Colony on Typhon, fascinating."**

content: Lexi doesn't say anything for a long moment. "**I'm afraid that to tell you everything would take more time than I actually have. Genetically modified Sith... they're called Reapers.. they're after whatever power they can find here in this place.**"

**"The Sith Have Returned!? Foreboding... Then again, I always knew they'd be back... Rest easy, child. At the very least, you seem to have found me first. Back in my day I personally lead a battalion of Jedi in the Fight Against the Brotherhood of Darkness. Other than what is contained in this room, there is nothing for the Sith *to find* within The Chapel of Placidity, except jungle predators and Trials Meant specifically to exclude Their Likes. This Mountain the final resting place of all Jedi Masters for a thousand High Generations. Unless the Sith aim is to exhume our dead for purposes of desecration, I doubt they'd find much... power, here."**

**"Apologies Child, I do not know of that planet."**

content: "**It's not a planet...**" she furrows her brow as she recalls how it was explained to her. "**Its a Realm of the Force. There space and time have no meaning. The masters on Tython sent me through a portal to the World Between Worlds and once there I was able to travel into the future to another world.**"
"**The Sith have been performing their own rituals, sending their acolytes into the past to corrupt history.**"

His eyebrows arch, **"Truly Fascinating. My colleagues had proposed theories, that such a Realm existed. If thou sayest true, their guesswork wasn't for naught."** Upon the turn of the subject towards the Sith, his eyebrow furrowed once more: **"Will those Charlatans never cease plaguing our Galaxy. How do you plan to stop them, Clan-kin?"**

author.username: Holoprojection of Jedi Master Gellendor Ardena, Nature-Chieftain of Clan Ardena

content: "**The Force has guided me to the time when the first traveler went back. When I stop him, then the threads of time will hopefully repair themselves... That's my hope at least.**"

**"Are we operating solely on Hopes and Guidance from the Force? Is the Fate of the Galaxy riding on such a flimsy current? Tsk tsk tsk... I sense you are very young and inexperienced. Who among Our Clan taught you the Ways of the Force?"**

author.username: Holoprojection of Jedi Master Gellendor Ardena, Nature-Chieftain of Clan Ardena
author.global_name: Holoprojection of Jedi Master Gellendor Ardena, Nature-Chieftain of Clan Ardena

content: "**No one among the clan... I was taught by Masters of the Academy.**" Lexi suddenly feels very foolish under the gaze of the Jedi Master. "**As I mentioned... there hasn't been a Jedi in our clan for many generations.**"
Frowning, **"I see... that is very discouraging. I suppose we'll have to rely on your training by these... Academy Masters as you say. Tell me one thing... did they teach you of The Verdant Force?"**

content: Lexi considers for a moment, wracking her memory of mention even in passing of such an aspect of the Force. Finally, she shakes her head. "**No, Master. I don't recall hearing about it, even in passing.**"
"**Unless you are to mean, the Living Force?**"

**“I suspect we are speaking with different vocabularies. I refer to specifically the Verdant Force. It’s an extraordinarily rare Legendary Jedi ability. Only members carrying within their blood the genetic heritage of the Verdant Bloodline are able to even glimpse the Verdant Force. Those of The Lineage are able to See the Currents of the Living Force. The Verdant Force, as it is sometimes called, is the ability to tap into the Living Currents of the Force, to enhance Life and cleanse Corruption. Our Bloodline, the Verdant Bloodline, is unique in all of the Jedi clans. It turns us into Perfect Bulwarks Against Corruption. We of the Clan Ardena uniquely specialize in cleansing and purging evil, even of the most dire kind. Hence, only one of the fold can truly guide, educate, and mold a member of the Verdant Heritage.”**

**"Among other things, yes. It allows us to perform healing, to talk to plants, to befriend animals, and to heal conflicts more broadly."**

content: "**Earlier today...**" she pauses as she tries to put into words her experience. Then in a rush as if just wanting to get the words out, confesses: **"I was fighting someone who was corrupted.. turned into part beast, part man. Instead of pushing him away with the Force, the plants around me grew larger and well... ripped him to pieces before devouring him.**"

One eyebrow raised: **"The veritable Verdant Force Push, impressive. If you can perform such a feat, you truly are who you say. However, judging by your wording, you seem to have no inkling of the Principles underpinning your recent experience. All of your striving was instinctual. Listen to me closely Child... You *must* Complete Your Training, otherwise you might misuse your gifts, and accidentally kill those you're aiming to heal. The Verdant Force is a two-edged blade, if misused for evil it can be just as malignant, as it can in the right hands, be benevolent."**

Smiling mirthfully: **"Oh Child, that is the *only* thing I can do in my current state."** The hologram nods to the body in repose. **"If you fervently wish to heal the Corrupted, you must first cleanse the Corruption in one's own Soul. If you follow the steps outlined in my Principles of Verdancy for Beginners, my published 3rd Edition Text, you too could begin to learn, the Ways of the Verdant Force. Of course reading is not enough, for knowledge must always be tempered with... experience."** He points to the shelf of dusty old books, just above the bed.

content: Lexi stands. Looking at them dubiously she wonders if they would even survive her breathing on them. She wanted to learn, but she had to temper her desire with the path laid ahead of her.  Gently, she reaches out to test whether the book would hold up to physical contact.

It seems that all but one book crumbles at her touch, turning to dust and falling apart nearly instantly. The one surviving textbook indeed has the title: *Principles of Verdancy I, 3rd Edition* It appears to be made of a Professional Grade material, not like the other texts which were all written on flimsy jungle leaves.

**"Well would you look at that, there goes all of my life's work. Tis a good thing all those texts are copied my holocron -- wait a moment, something is the matter... Calculating..."** His eyes grow distant, then return to life-like: **"There appears to be a block placed my archives. Of course, I neglected to explain, this power over Verdancy is not simply for anyone, not even for any Clan member of Ardena. The Verdant Force must be eternally guarded above that of all other Jedi Secrets."**

At his explanation she looks back at him. "**I understand. I presume once I show mastery of certain skills then you'd be able to teach me more.**"

Principles of Verdancy I teaches the reader the following powers: Verdant Healing I (owned), Animal Empathy I (owned), Verdant Force Push I (owned), Stone Shield I, Diagnose Corruption I, Verdant Growth I and Verdant Purification I. The new Force Powers can be picked up as options during leveling.

**"Not just that, Child, but... I don't want to overwhelm you. Take up a meditative position on the roof and begin your studies. Pick a Power you wish to learn, read the Chapter that corresponds to it, and then come to me when you're ready to practice it together."**

**"Reasonably sure yes. Not for a while at least. If you wish to take extra precautionary steps, however, you could take my book and holocron to another, safer, location."**

content:
Lexi shakes her head at the suggestion. "**There is nowhere safer. They took my ability to command the ship we came on. I'm trapped on Dantooine until I confront them.**" She knew NZ would be able to pilot the ship, but she didn't want to trust the droid was willing to follow her orders. Without the rank plate, she'd have no control over the ship once they left.  Sighing she stands and takes both the book as the Holocron over to the dusty table. Setting them aside and pulls rations from her bag and begins to munch on them, contemplating. 
"**Do you know anything about Force Bonds? How they're created or... Severed?**" She pauses as she takes a small sip of her water. "**A bond was created between myself and one of the Sith after me while we were in the World Between Worlds. He's demonstrated on occasion he can read my mind. He only seems to be able to do so when he's in balance with himself, which he very much is not now.**'

**"Corruption is subtle at first, until it comes to a full crest. If you managed to forge a Force Bond, you should manage to sever it. The stronger the Bond, the harder the process of disconnecting it. How strong of a bond did you forge, with this Sith?"**

Eyebrows shoot up. **"Oh."** Pause. **"I see... Well in that case it may be... rather difficult, to sever this bond. There are fewer bonds more entangled than the bonds of love. This is why Jedi do not form attachments."**

content:
Lexi raises her own eyebrow at that and with a shrug collects her things. With the book in hand and the holocrin secure in a pouch of her belt, she heads up to the roof. 
Once there she surveys her surroundings. If nothing else, she'd have a better vantage for when the Sith appeared. Flipping through the pages she finds the chapter she was interested in and begins the instruction.

Lexi enters the Serene Sanctum, the rocky clearing at the top of the roof, which makes a perfectly flat surface, ideal for practicing martial arts poses. The Jedi imagines she would also feel at ease trying to levitate with legs crossed at the apex. Settling in, the Textbook seems to float weightlessly in the air, pages turning. As the pages turn, blue hieroglyphs highlight words in the Dome of the Ceiling, which correspond to the words in the text, different parts of the Dome lighting up on synchronized lit sequence. The pages accelerate as they fly past, flipping faster and faster. Flashing lights cause a veritable Light Show to appear on the Ceiling of the Dome in synchronization with the words being opened in the book. Just as suddenly as it had started, the book flapped shut, the glow extinguished. Lexi can feel that somehow the words of the book have been burned into her mind, and she can re-read any of them at will.

Lexi is blinded by the lights as each inscription imprints itself into her mind. She's reminded suddenly of her experience within the World Between Worlds when the images and impressions inscribed themselves into her memory then too. 
When it feels as though her head was about to burst, the transfer completes and she's left breathless, her head tilted upward towards the dome. 
With relief she closes her eyes and takes in calming breaths. As her thoughts turn to the new knowledge she realizes that somehow she'd been instinctively using the Verdant Force, much as Gellandor had surmised. Now though, she had a basis of understanding on how her new found abilities worked. 
Slowly, she withdraws the Holocron from her pouch and call forth the guardian. "**I'm ready.**" She says.

**"Very good, not at all bad for your first time in fact. If you have any books you need to learn, this is the ideal place to Speed Read them using the Force. Also, you'll find most Force powers work better from this Focus Point. If you can find other Focus Points by tracking down the Currents of the Living Force and following them to their Focal Nodes, you'll be able to discover other Focus Points around the Galaxy, I'm sure."**

content: Lexi looks at him blankly. "**A self-whats it?**" Her tone grows more somber. "**Yes about 30 years ago a Sith lord overthrew the Republic and began an annihilation campaign against the Jedi... Politically and physically.  More than 10,000 Jedi died in the first year alone. Turned on by the soldiers they had been trusting to watch their back.**" She pauses. "**I did warn you that there was a lot to discuss than time may allow.**"
Gasps. **"No~! Not the Republic, gone..."**

content:
"**Wait!**" Lexi calls trying to keep the guardian from leaving just yet. "**What about locking the book?**" She gives an exasperated sigh as he disappears. 
Putting the Holocron back into her pouch she turns to carefully wrapping the book into her robe and then placing it into her bag. It was going to have to be a problem for another time. Gazing about, she leans back in the middle of the arena. Looking up at the multitude of inscriptions, she allows her mind to wander and eventually dozes off.
8 hours later, you reawaken, the smell of the Waterfall is fresh and inspiring. You feel your spirits well rested, more rested than normal, even.

Lexi stretches out, her back creaking from sleeping on the hard surface. Having not intending on falling asleep she silent curses herself for her lack of sense. However, she feels better than she has in a long time. Not since she had left Tython has she felt so well rested, so calm. It was a balm to her soul after everything which had happened. 
Slowly she sits up and tries to gauge how long she has been asleep. Ever since Vaz had knocked her out, she'd lost all sense of time. In this place though she didn't quite care.
She pulls out more rations and her canteen and begins to eat, not wanting to break the spell she had found. It would come soon enough.
Venturing outdoors, it is evident that Morning has arrived on this planet, as the fog lifts to reveal the surrounds of the Waterfall guarding the Serene Sanctum.

As the early morning light begins to burn away the fog, Lexi decides she's tarried long enough. Checking over her belongings once moreand descends from the roof. Taking a final look around the domicile she heads towards the waterfall  to make her way out of the temple.

Nothing has changed overnight, in fact, nothing seems to have changed over the past few centuries here. The humidity of the waterfall allows for lichen and barnacles to grow on the walls, slowly inchig upwards over the course of centuries.
Looking out across the jungle from atop the waterfall, she sees a majestic view of the Chapel of Placidity:
Unfortunately, she is facing the inaccessible back of the Temple, as it seems she will have to travel a ways to reach the stairs.
She takes a final look, transcribing the place to her memory before turning away and starting her trek through the Temple.

Descending from the outcropping after passing through the waterfall, she takes in the view of the clearing before her and the majestic Chapel. Spotting the ruins not to far off, she decides to get a look at them on her way around the base of the Chapel.
Without realizing it, Lexi had missed a peculiar shadow resting underneath the shade of the Ruin as she passed it on her way across the Jungle floor to the opposite side of the Chapel.

Now, the shadow rose up and revealed itself to be a monstrous ferile feline, with mutant spikes protruding every which way. The Dark Side emanated openly from this beast, its eyes glowing red. It opens its mouth and hisses:

As the beast lunges in its attack, Lexi reflexively calls on the Force and by using the beast's momentum against it sends it hurling over her head, a swipe of a heavy paw just missing her head. 

Rebounding, the beast lunges at her and leaves three gashes along her back as she turns in her attempt to avoid its claws. With a snap hiss her saber ignites and as she finishes her turn she shears off several of the dark spikes. 

Smelling blood the beast redoubles the attack, but prepared this time, Lexi meets the creature's claws with an attack of her own. She removes more spikes from the creature's tough hide.

Further incensed, the beast gets through Lexi's defenses as it charges her and shreds her back further with more bloody slashes. Focusing through the pain, Lexi counters the attack and lands a blow to the dark beast's shoulder.

Drawing on the Force, Lexi suddenly anticipates the beast's next move and with perfect precision as it comes in for a deadly bite with its massive maw skewers the dark feline through its maw.

Breathing heavily as she stands over the creature, she gasps with the pain in her back as the adrenaline begins to fade. Moving away several steps from the corpse she drops her pack to the ground and rummages around for the medpac she had brought with her. At the very least to stem the bleeding.
After a few minutes of applying bacta bandages Lexi spots another Mutant Beast across the pond, lazily lapping water with it's tongue.
The water around it turns black with tendrils of infected-looking saliva.

Lexi looks between the corpse of the beast she had just killed and at the one across the pond. Realization dawns as they corruption from the beast spreads into the pond. These creatures had been created by the machinations of the two Reapers, who had likely grown tired of the maze and traps presented by the Temple and were now trying to corrupt it.
Warily, she collects her pack and heads towards the creature. She's going to have to deal with it and any thing else corrupted before the whole oasis is destroyed.
With her saber in hand, she calls on the Verdant Force and pushes the creature back away from the pond.
Massive tree trunks crush the mutant to death, popping jt in a gush of purple blood. Afterwards, Lexi spots a healthy feline approach the pond, making ready to drink the waters within.

Lexi looks between the area of the pond where the corrupted creature had been drinking and then to the healthy feline. Was the water tainted? She doesn't take the chance as she leaps out and attempts to startle the feline from taking a drink.

As the creature startles away she turns back to the pond. If the water was now contaminated by the Dark Side then it had to be dealt with before any more creatures fell to the corruption. Bending down she dips her hand slowly into the water, and uses her newfound knowledge to purify the water.

A soft green glow coalesces from her fingertips and begins to spread out into the water of the pond, the black tendrils from the taint recoiling before disipating from the touch. Gazing into the pond, Lexi's reflection gazes back at her, the bright cyan glow of her eyes a stark contrast to the dimming verdant glow of the water. A comforting warmth spreads up her arm and through her body and she sighs contentedly as she withdraws it.
The task completed, Lexi looks about for any more unfortunate creatures which had succumbed to the corruption.
None are visible at this time.

Determining there was no more corrupted beasts in this area, she continues on her journey towards the front of the Chapel. She remians dilligent for any more creatures and the Reapers who had created them.
