content: Lexi walks around the periphery of the 100 meter tall citadel walls, and finally the large staircase comes into view. It has 100 steps exactly.
At the top of the stairs, stands an imposing figure. A muscled Sith, arms folded, standing with both feet planted on a decorative statue, one of two flanking the top of the imposing stairwell.
The Sith's dark side signature is like a red flame burning off Wraith to the Jedi's cyan eyes.
**"YOU"** His red eyes catch her's.
The organic Charybdis spikes weren't evident, as they were the last time she had glimpsed him.
Lexi gazes up at the dark, dominating figure atop the statue at the entrance of the Chapel proper. "**Surprised?**" she asks evenly. "**Did you find what you were looking for?**"
**"YES. YOU ARE NO LONGER NEEDED, AND CAN DIE NOW."** He holds out a new lightsaber handle.
Double-bladed red saber ignites.

Lexi is unpeterbed by the development and looks up at this dark version of Wraith with determination. "**I wish it hadn't come to this, Wraith. It would seem promises from a Sith mean nothing after all.**" Her own lightsaber ignites, casting its emerald light on her face.
**"YES, HATE ME."** Using the Force, he flicks a giant boulder, at Lexi, in the middle of the sentence.

As the boulder flies at Lexi, she dives, executing a roll and hearing a giant crash behind her as the 1-ton boulder impacts against a tree. As nimbly as one of the jungle predators, Lexi bounds up the stairwell. Wraith, awaiting her at the top begins to dance and weave a flurry of lightsaber swipes. One of which makes it past Lexi’s defenses leaving a nasty cautery burn. In return, Lexi manages to slip in 2 deadly strikes of her own, one to the neck and one to the heart. Wraith’s body is marred by burn marks, but he seems not to notice. Similar to Xod, his skin had somehow become tough and partly lightsaber-resistant. A cold mechanically-modified laugh emits from the Deathmask, as the Sith had hopped back, assessing the skill of his opponent.
**"POINTLESS, I CAN SIMPLY OUTLAST YOU"**
**"WHY NOT JOIN ME INSTEAD?"**
"**Join you.**" she says in a deadpan. "**To what end?**"
**"IMMORTALITY. POWER. CONTROL."** Slowly, dramatically, bony spikes emerge from his back.
"**Is that what Vazz'ri has promised you?**"
**"PROMISED? I ALREADY HAVE IT"**
A bony spike flies through the air at Lexi.
Dark Wraith had concealed a spike slowly emerging from his inner wrist, flicking it at Lexi as he was attempting to distract her.
The needle-thin spike embeds itself in Lexi's flesh, spearing her through, and foiling her lightning fast lightsaber strike.

Twirling his saberstaff in a helicoptering motion 360 degrees around his waist, he deflects her attack and comes out of the dance, standing on one foot, balancing in anticipation of the next exchange.
**"AFTER I CUT OFF YOUR LIMBS, THAT'S WHEN THE FUN BEGINS."**

Lexi summons the Force, as plants from the ground spring forth to spear through the Sith, but once more his helicoptering defense lawnmowers through the deadly plants. A few wooden shards do land though, one spearing through the Sith's guts.
Lexi pulls out the spike from her flesh and charges in the wake of the deadly plants.

Timing it just right, Lexi's saber comes in with some speed and strength, and at such a perfect angle, as to score a perfect strike against Wraith's chest, burning a red slash across it. Wraith's body begins to rebel on itself, joints wiggling and spikes emerging from everywhere, especially from the new wound. Wraith begins to transform into a partial Homunculus. His torso and limbs thicken, mass enlarging, back hunching and two long giant bony spikes emerging from his knuckles. They take the shape of two long, curved sickles. Wraith drops his saber and tests the new spikes against each other, producing sparks.
**"THANK YOU"**
Lexi and Wraith spar against one another, the spikes are sliced to pieces by swipes of Lexi's lightsaber, both dropping to the ground.
Wraith regrows the spikes, this time one scythe from each finger.
**"YOU CAN'T WIN"**

Lexi forms her hands into flat palms, holding both straight ahead of her, thumbs and index fingers touching. Winds flare, billowing her hair and buffeting her tunic. She begins to feel Wraith’s soul, focusing everything upon it. Gradually tearing her hands apart, she can feel Wraith’s soul becoming disjointed at the same time. The Sith Monstrosity levitates off the ground, paralyzed within the Jedi’s grip and seemingly in anguish. As the Force Purification ends, it drops to the ground in a heap, landing nimbly on its feet. It’s dual red glowing eyes narrow evilly, then go wide with surprise, as the Dark Creature before her suddenly heaves and vomits a leathery body bag made of a semi-transparent see-through membrane, holding a naked muscular body within. Back paddling on its haunches, the creature screeches an unnatural alien howl of hate and anguish. It begins to wiggle uncontrollably, transforming itself into a full Mark VI Homunculus, otherwise known as a Villelaeus type. It towers, growling and exhaling a horrid breath of warm humid eye-watering death. Narrowing its glowing red gaze, it leaps at Lexi, swiping with a terribly misshapen claw-hand.

As the creature arrives in a blur of deathly spikes, Lexi's cyan eyes flare, slowing time down to the millisecond. Slobbering wet ropes of saliva stretch through the air, as Lexi weaves and bobs in between them with preternatural agility, leaving behind a streak of cyan as she twists and turns, swinging off the Beast's claws like a gymnast, performing double flips and running up its arm, to somersault onto its back and driving her green lightsaber in through the back of the Beast's head, the very tip of the green blade poking out in between the beasts burning yellow-red eyes. The green blade retracts and the eyes extinguish like two fires dying simultaneously. The massive Sith Creature crashes to the ground, Lexi riding out the crash on top of its back. A tremendous BOOM is heard, as the Mark VI falls, and a moderate plume of dust billows outwardly in a circle for a brief moment. In the distance, birds caw and take off from a tree. All is quiet once more now, action halting as violently as it had begun.
A solitary figure stands.

Lexi breathes heavily as she lands in a crouch, her saber extended behind her. She turns as she hears the creature hit the ground with enough force to shake the ground. 
Standing, she turns her attention to the translucent sack the homunculus had expelled.
The figure is buck naked, emerging from a clean slice through the membranous sack, dripping with alien fluids and wielding a brilliant bright cyan blade.
Exiting from a combat pose, the figure's stance relaxes. It regards its surroundings, with some curiosity.
Seeing Lexi astride the corpse of a massive beast of some kind, he raises one eyebrow questioningly.
**"Okay what did I miss?"**

Lexi moves away from the beast and strides towards the reborn Wraith. "**I don't know all of it... But Vaz injected up with the Charybdis serum.**" She turns and looks at the creature.  "**I was able to split that part of it from you... I guess you could say I just killed your darker half or at least the corrupted part.**"
**"That is... shocking, to say the least. I do feel, lighter, somehow..."**
He regards his limbs, looking down and realizing he was undressed, his cheeks burn.
Seeing him blush she grins at him. "**Modesty... Now? It's not like I haven't seen you as such before.**" All things considered, she couldn't help but find some levity in his predicament
His body was unblemished, as if born yesterday, veins cording up and down his arms flexing effortlessly.
**"I'm sorry to have been apart so long... it seems we have a traitor in our midst."**
He bends down and tears a sheet of rough, hardened skin off the dead creature's side, using his saber to cut it into a kilt and cloak, complete with hood and cape.
**"This will have to make do, for now."**

"**Do you remember anything that's happened since we last spoke? I feel like I missed a lot when I went into the trance before we arrive on Dantooine 
**"

His cheeks burn red once more, looking away. **"Uh, yes... My recollection is, somewhat... hazy. All I remember is... Going to sleep, and when I awoke Vaz had injected me with something, the same color that Xod held in his laboratory. After that, I remember rage, and then nothing."**

"**Kryze told me she is a Reaper... She must be the apprentice Xod told us about.**" She shakes her head. "**How she managed to get a hold of his serum though is beyond me. She managed to inject him as well.**" She adds the last slowly, hoping Wraith would understand the ultimate result of his transformation and his demise.
Slowly, she moves towards him. "**The pregnancy was a ruse as well. I think so she could get close to us.**"
Wraith's eyes dart back and forth, processing the information: **"She has be a Reaper Envoy, she must have hidden her Force Signature from us all this time."**
Stopping his gaze on Lexi, he raises one eyebrow: **"What could she be hoping to obtain?"**
'**I don't know.  Perhaps we should find out?**" She turns and looks at the Chapel entrance.
At the very center of the Citadel, stands a massive dome-shaped cupula-like structure, having one open arching double-doorway, it appears completely dark within.

Approximately 100 meters tall, the dome is perfectly round, made of ancient dull-orange Temple Stone, and inscribed with rows of hieroglyphs and bas reliefs, showing ancient Jedi of the High Republic, in various poses of lightsaber combat and meditation.
**"Great, let's go kill us a Reaper bitch."**
Lexi nods in agreement beside Wraith. "**She has a lot to answer for,**" she says evenly before heading towards the lone archway

Inside, the Dome is supported by a row of support columns running in a circle at the periphery. In the center, a statue of a massive High Republic-dressed Jedi holding up the Dome as if generating his own massive Force bubble. Also in the middle of the room is a large tree, albino white and sending branches throughout the whole room, that each grow into the roof and around the statue’s legs and torso. The Albino Tree sprouts white leaves, and atop the branches are a small army of waiting Mutant Beasts: 9 pairs of red eyes narrowing menacingly, several of the felines hissing at the newcomers. Atop the massive hooded head of the statue, sitting cross-legged, you immediately spot Doctor Vazz'ri De L'est, recognizable by twin head-tails. She looks… different. Her entire demeanor has changed, now appearing somehow outwardly more demonic and in her hands tapping a pink shortsaber impatiently. From far above, laughter echoes: **"Well, well, well look who decided to come to play. I hope you didn’t think this would be a fair fight?"**
Lexi gazes about at the corrupted felines and then to the Twi'lek. "**You certianly work fast, Vaz. How long had you been holding onto the serum?**"

**"Long enough. I couldn't let such a potent power simply go to waste. As soon as you told me of the self destruct on Hyperion, I decided to hide a small sample on my person.... you'll never guess where. Anyway, your leading us straight to this High Republic Burial Site was just the cherry on top. I got rid of that egomaniac Xod, at last, and thanks to you, I have finally Completed his Dark Alchemical Quest, thanks to the added DNA of every single Jedi buried here, Project Charybdis is now complete, and with that... your services are no longer required."** Her head-tails grow out longer, into curved horns and eyes glowing yellow pits. **"I am now the Ultimate Shapeshifter. Once my pets devour you, I can take your shape and leave this planet, taking on any guise I desire... the Galaxy shall be my Plaything. Tell me, Jedi, how does it feel knowing that your actions have doomed this Galaxy forever? Yes... "** Her body stretches its torso to half-again its height. **"Your pain is delicious to me, your mental anguish the sweetest ambrosia..."**

"**Congratulations... you've managed to surpass your master in the melodramatic and crazy department.**" Lexi says, apparently unimpressed by the Shapeshifter's confession. "**There's a problem with your plan... You have to kill us first.**"
She erupts in a diabolical laugh, sharp teeth parting to reveal a devilish forked tongue.
**"Die, Jedi~!"** The felines pounce forwards.

As nine of the Mutant Beasts leap off the albino branches, Lexi balls her fists and releases a wave of green verdant energy into the room, awakening the branches into writhing snakes that bend and crush 3 of the beasts, popping them into pulp. The other 6 split off into two groups of 3, each group going after Lexi and Wraith.

Wraith attempt to send a wave of Light Side energy to overwhelm Vaz, who meets it with a massive wave of red Dark Side Power. The two Reapers strain against each other, eventually Wraith's power breaks, leaving Vaz uneffected and smiling widely, eyes sparkling hungrily. She crooks a finger at the male Reaper, beckoning him to come closer.

Back on the ground, Lexi has dispatched 2 of the 3 beasts facing her with expert acrobatic strikes. Wraith runs up the white branches, hopping ever higher with each bounding hop and sprint. 3 Mutant Beasts hound his steps, following him in a line of chasing spikes.
Lexi bends her knees, as she becomes a single blur of cyan and emerges from the opposite side of the last Mutant Beast, having sliced it cleanly in half.

Vaz' body has elongated into a long pink snake. Wrapping around the tree, she takes swipes at Wraith, as he jumps to dodge her tail swipes. The single forked tongue of the Giant Mutant Snake holds the shortsaber in its forked grip.

The cyan blur which had sliced the Mutant Beast down the middle, streaking forward and up the tree trunk, as one foot after another Lexi ran up the trunk of the massive central albino tree, seemingly without effort, in a flash of cyan lightning, ending up flanking Vaz together with Wraith. Lexi’s eyes meet Wraith’s and time once more slows for a brief instant, as they communicate innately through their Force Bond, inside of a split second, instinctually realizing a way to coordinate their attacks from opposite angles. Wraith and Lexi instinctively move into position in unison, on opposite sides of the Giant Mutated Pink Serpent. As Vaz goes to chomp down on Wraith, he had perfectly positioned himself as a target for the Snake’s Two Colossal Fangs, blocking the bite with his cyan lightsaber, allowing Lexi to strike in perfect unison from the opposite flank and decapitate the Snake’s head with her green blade. The body wiggles lifelessly and drops down, draped on the branches of the tree, while the head falls down to the white leafy-covered floor, landing in a heap. Landing on the ground, squealing from pain, the head still wiggles its forked tongue wielding the pink shortsaber, somehow alive, slowly regenerating, forming tiny legs like that of a tadpole, it begins waddling away out of sight. Meanwhile, two of the Mutant Beasts each attack Lexi and Wraith, though both get cut down in a fury. The last feline beast is busy fighting Wraith. On the ground, Lexi sees a newly born naked Doctor Vaz Twi'lek body, climbing out of the mouth of the snake, the Twi'lek naked and wet, dripping alien juices. The Doctor stands up, wielding her pink blade, no longer smiling but holding a simmering anger.

Vaz eyes flash with insanity and she strikes. Her arm stretches around unnaturally long, shooting out of her shoulder like a ravenous snake, going all the way around and skewering Lexi through the back of your torso, pink blade bursting out of Lexi’s chest and lifting her up, closer to Vaz as the Reaper grins maniacally, eyes glittering. Lexi begins to feel herself grow unconscious. Hovering in the air before Vaz, impaled on one gangly limb, the Jedi feels her life begin to extinguish, as the Reaper holds out Lexi like a prize catch.

All of a sudden, to interrupt the moment, Wraith leaps downward and slashes the Shapeshifter down the middle from collarbone to between her legs. Vaz's surprise is evident as she collapses into two halves. As Vaz hits the floor, she screeches and her DNA begins to unravel, much like Xod’s, turning into grey dust that cracks, flakes off, and flies away into the wind.

Lexi slumps as the pink saber automatically shuts off, black cauterized hole gaping in her chest. Wraith catches Lexi in his arms, as she collapses into them, eyes fluttering closed. Lexi’s last act before falling unconscious is to summon the last vestige of Force she was collecting in her soul, and releasing it at the ancient albino tree centering the chamber. After Lexi’s mind went dim, everything halted. A pregnant pause hung in the air. Then, after what almost felt like an interminable eternity, the Ancient Tree pulsed, once. Then pulsed stronger, for a second time. Then, stronger still for a third time. Lexi’s heart began to beat in her chest once more. Lexi’s eyes opened wide in shock, surprised to see her wound had already knit closed. She took a shuddering gasp of air into her lungs, and found them to be functional again. Wraith, holding her in his arms, was just as shocked in response. The tree had disappeared into the Force Dimension, fading away until only an afterimage remained, and then disappearing altogether. Lexi had passed out after this. Behind the scenes, Wraith had searched the Chapel and found a small Science Station, where he collected Vaz’ research and retrieved Lexi’s rank plate.

Relaxing into Wraith’s arms, Lexi felt the world come alive once more, as Wraith walked out of the Cupula, back out the double doors, after some undetermined amount of time had passed. The sunshine was once more upon their faces, as they stood atop the Chapel of Placidity, stairs in front of them, and lush jungle all around. Above them in the sky, at the apex, a familiar shape was growing closer. The Antioch was gradually descending to land atop the Chapel, next to the Cupula. With a hiss, the ramp had deployed. Wraith walked towards the descended boarding ramp, carrying Lexi all the while.

Lexi is only semi aware as Wraith carries her from the Chapel into the bright light of the oasis jungle. The shock from her near death experience leaves her feeling out of touch with reality and only gradually does the world seem to come alive and into focus. She blinks dazedly as she feels the heat of the sun touch her face. Nestled against Wraith’s chest, she closes her eyes and just allows herself to feel the life around her. 

The hiss of the ramp brings Lexi back to herself and her head comes up off Wraith’s chest as she looks around. “**Wait… we can’t… can’t leave till we’re sure.**” She says, her words coming out tentatively at first, but grows stronger with her resolve. Her fist grabs at the collar of his cloak. “**We can’t leave any taint here to fester.**”

Wraith stops in his tracks, looking down at the soft pleading voice in his arms. He smiles gently, serene look on his face. Then, upon realizing the content of Lexi's words, his back straightened out: **"I thought about that too. After collecting Xod's research, and burning it along with the mutated corpses, I called in N-Zee using my retrieved rank plate to exfiltrate us from the planet... What else would you have me do? Do you know of some way to detect any more remains of Project Charybdis?"**

Lexi takes a long moment to consider her answer, her mind still a fog. The contents of the book inscribed into her memory and their incumbent inscriptions flash across her thoughts.  "**Know, yes... But not the means.**"
**"I see, well in that case, there is nothing more to be done. Are you ready to leave Dantooine, or do you plan to stay here a while longer, Captain?"**

Casting her glance about, Lexi looks upon the last of the jungle she can see. "**I haven't felt such calm anywhere since I left Tython,**" she says softly as if talking to herself. Her head falls back to rest on Wraith's chest, comforted by his warmth and presence. "**You're right... there's nothing that can be done right now.**"

**"Actually... that's not exactly true. The lower levels of this large temple structure appear vast and unexplored. Once you're feeling up to it, we could venture deeper down to see if there is anything left of Project Charybdis. Right now, I need to get you to the Med Bay and help you recover. We're in no shape to delve the Catacombs after the fight we just barely survived. Please, rest..."** He gently runs his hand along her face and cheek.

N-Zee approaches the pair, after clanking down the boarding ramp. **"Hasty Arrival: I have cleaned up the interior, Captain, Lieutenant. I understand there was some urgency, and have already revved up the Auto-Doc at the Med Bay."**

Wraith seems slightly irritated at the interruption of what was, at least partially, a tender shared moment. **"Yes, N-Zee, let's get Lexi inside and patched up. Don't take off just yet, there might be a small follow up excursion before we depart."**

The idea isn't unappealing but with the mention of medbay her expression changes. She pushes the thoughts down while Wraith is distracted by NZ. "**Let's have the Auto-Doc look at me so I can clean up and get some sleep.**"

Continuing up the ramp, the males get Lexi dual IVs started in both arms and begin infusing life support fluids. The Jedi passes out on the Medical Bed, as bright fluorescent lights blind out all vision. After 8 hours of rest and intensive Bacta Infusions, Bacta Bandages, and Bacta Shots, Lexi's eyes open once more to the comforting hum of the Antioch's life support systems. She feels fully rested and recovered, as well as having a new unexplained feature. Her chest wound has not regrown entirely it seemed, but instead something was growing outwardly from within. A small white twig has emerged, topped with a single violet-purple-gradient leaf. It faces the fluorescent lights above, hungrily gulping up radiant light energy. Lexi can feel the energy from the lights, travelling down and into her body, and helping to speed up her recovery. As she sits up, tentatively, the twig flicks back into her body, skin healing over and leaving no trace of the strange plant. Lexi has to blink and rub her eyes, to wonder whether what she just witnessed was a potentially trauma-conjured hallucination, drug-induced delirium, or something else entirely.

Lexi stares dumbfounded at the healed would on her chest as the purple leafed tree disappears without a trace inside her. Still hooked up to the pair of IV lines, she slowly sits up and gingerly touches the spot. Convinced it's the delirium from the trauma she's recently endured, she puts it to the back of her mind. Glancing around, she starts to carefully pull out the IVs keeping her from moving freely.

Two warm hands grasp her shoulders and place her gently, but firmly on her back once more. Wraith's upside-down face appears, eyes locking with Lexi's. **"Careful now, I don't want you getting up too soon and passing out from the sudden blood pressure drop."** His lips hover tantalizingly close to her own, moisture evident and suddenly Lexi feels parched, her own lips dry and almost cracked from the recent ordeal.
"**I'll take it slow,**" Lexi promises, her voice hoarse. She holds his gaze for a long moment, his cyan gaze meeting her own. "**I'm thirsty, do you have any water?**"
**"Dinner just got done reheating. Care to join me in the Galley? I'll carry you there, and I won't brook any objections."**
Lexi gives him an eye roll and decides to object anyways. "**I can walk... I feel fine.. better than fine actually.**"
**"Ah-- Of course, Captain, I'm sorry--."** He blushes and releases her from his grasp, reluctantly. **"I'm glad... you're feeling better."**
Inside the Galley, atop the bar, a large party-ration had been unpacked, reheated, and prepped for consumption.
**"Dig in, Captain. I figured you'd be hungry."**
There was a large bowl of soup, multiple protein sources, sides, deserts, and multiple drink selections.

Lexi regrets her decision when he pulls away from her and falls back on formalities. "**What happened to calling me Lexi?**" she decides to ask. Slowly, as promised, she sits up and takes her time finding her feet. 
Eventually, she makes her way to the galley without passing out from light headedness and takes a seat at the bar. "**You've pulled out the veritable feast,**"  Lexi grins at him in appreciation and pulls towards her one of the fruit juices. "**I'm starved.**" After taking a sip of the sugary drink she eyes the rest of the food trying to decide where to start. **" How did you know when I would wake to have everything ready?**"

Blushing deeper at her question, he turns away. **"Did I say that out loud? I'm terribly sorry Captain, I'll-- strive to express more professionalism in my conduct. This way please, *ma'am*..."** Standing, he still doesn't meet her gaze, preferring to stare at any another object nearby Lexi instead. After guiding her to the bar, and taking a seat alongside, he answered her question as matter-of-factly as he could muster: **"Auto-Doc said your REM sleep cycle was winding down and estimated you'd be awake in 45 minutes. Ma'am. Just in time it seems..."**

Deciding to make a game of it, she pulls the bowl of soup her way. "**Well, *Lt.*... It all looks very good.**" While she takes a spoonful she looks him over. "**What have you been up to while I've been sleeping the day away?**"
**"Just trying to undo the mess and bring the ship back to some semblance of order. Captain, I think, we should probably avoid taking on any more passengers from now on, if at all possible..."**

Lexi winces at the reproach and then looks around. When she had left there was an intense garden growing, fertilized by the remains of the late Breggor Kryze. "**I'll do my best, but I don't make any promises. We don't seem to have a very good track record for making new friends... And now we're going to explain to Thrawn why his man is dead...**"

**"I just hope he doesn't mind, if we uh... lighten up the story a bit, to say that Breggor Kryze valiantly protected us from certain death, at the hands of hostile Reapers... and managed to help us finally locate Mount Tantis. That should take the sting out of the bad news, don't you think?"** One eyebrow raised, he said the last words unnaturally seductive: **"*Captain*."**

Lexi's own eyebrow shoots up in response to his seductive tone. She takes a breath to restrain herself as a blush begins to form on her cheeks. He was making a game if it too, she realizes. "**Let's just hope he never had a chance up send in a report that we didn't tell him right away about Weyland. Will have to check what messages went out.**'

Wraith is intent upon examining a piece of ripe fruit that had been rehydrated up to full salaciousness. Keeping one eye brow raised, he alternated looking between Lexi and the ripe fruit: **"I would also hope, that he wasn't sending out, any, uh, *mixed messages*, shall we call them..."** He suddenly took a vicious chomp and chewed on the piece of fruit slowly, keeping strong eye contact.

Lexi watches him intently, her mouth going dry once more and an ache growing with her. "**He did seem more a plain, to the point sort...**" Suddenly she blinks and turns her head away from his gaze. "**If you're going to keep trying to seduce me, we have something to discuss first.**"

Wraith sharply takes in a bite of air. **"I keep forgetting. The... uh, shall we say, *unintended consequences* of coupling, often slip my mind, when I'm intent upon my goals too narrowly."** He shrugs, gesturing: **"Why don't we just try to get along, and avoid any *internal eruptions* from now on, going forward? I'm sure you could... use a *facial treatment* or two once in a while, right? I would only be too happy to oblige you, personally."**

Lexi looks at him sideways as he gives his suggestion, his turn of phrase leaving her blushing. "**When you're intent, will you forget then too? You seem biologically hardwired to find the most satisfaction from it. You've said yourself.... External stimulation won't be enough.**"

**"I don't intend to forgo anything internal, not at all... I only meant to say, that I should... *finish* my business somewhere less... restrictive."** He bites his lower lip, leaning in, smiling as he slurps up the last bit of fruit, swallowing hungrily.
There is a bit of fruit juice still coating his lips, as his breath smells fresh and sparkly.

As he leans in towards her, Lexi's body instinctively responds and she turns slightly to face him once more. Deliberately, she reaches up, her fingers slowly caress his lips where the juice from the fruit lingered. Her gaze intent on his, she brings her fingers to her mouth and sucks the juice from them. "**As long as we're on the same page,**" she says slowly.

**"I think... we might be..."** He leans in, kissing her full on the lips. Moaning softly as he licks her lips like a puppy briefly, then resumes kissing her, necking deep down and taking a plunge into her soft lips, grasping her by the skull with vicious need. The scene wipes across the screen and we see the following morning. Early dew and fog is burned away as the Dantooine Star announces dawn. Far away cawing is heard and a flock of avians flies in the distance. The buzz of jungle insects and the hooting and hollering of howler monkey-lizards high up the the branches. Wraith and Lexi walk down the boarding ramp, overlooking the jungle paradise. She is covered by a bedsheet across her chest for modesty, while Wraith has a sheet draped around his waist. Both are holding morning mugs of tea, and are halfway down the boarding ramp, letting the ship air out and get some fresh, plant-manufactured oxygen.
**"Sleep well?"**

Lexi sips at her tea looking over the jungle before them, taking in deep breaths of the dew filled air. At his question she raises an eyebrow, it seemed to have been an evening of anything but sleeping. "**Better for the company,**" she says suggestively. Adjusting the sheet she finds a soft patch of grass and moss to sit on. "**And yourself? Did you sleep at all?**"

**"Enough."** Preferring to remain standing, he sips on his mug of dark brew, and attempts to relax this morning. Puffs of steam come out between his lips, into the cool morning air. **"Gotta say this place is starting to grow on me. Speaking of... There's a whole hell of a lot of maze-like passageways beneath the first level where we fought Vaz. Seems to stretch on for a long ways, could be kilometers deep. I wonder if anything down there would be worthwhile gathering."**

Smiling obliquely Lexi hides it in her own cup. She shifts until she's sitting with her legs folded in front of her and she leans back on one arm.  Casting a glance up at Wraith she tells him what she knows. "**The Temple was used to test Jedi and ward off Sith... there could be anything down there. It was also used for Burials, which is how Vaz was able to get so much genetic materials.**"
**"Seems she isn't the only one around here intent on collecting genetic material."** He sips into his mug covering his expression.
Lexi ignore the jibe. "**Speaking of... What did you do with the research?**"

**"Destroyed it. Burned along with the bodies. I hope that was all of it, but I got the impression that someone had offloaded it on a data disc, by the time I got to it. Can't be sure I destroyed the only copy..."**

"**This place is abandoned... It wasn't either of us and if it was on Vaz you would have found it... Who else could it have been?**" The last question is rhetorical as she thinks it over. "**Maybe we'd find out in the catecombs.**"
**"If the data was swiped it would also explain why there were so many bottles missing from the Science Station. I had hoped it was all used up by Vaz, but I couldn't say for sure..."**
"**Bottles? Of the serum?**" A pit begins to grow in her stomach as the idea of more of the Charybdis was still at large in the Galaxy.

**"While we're on the subject of threats... I gotta say I'm not sure we killed my Darker Half completely. First of all, the Death Mask has disappeared. Second, the massive monster we fought outdoors together... When I came out to set it ablaze, the back was split open like a locust's. Something has crawled out. Maybe it survived it's birth... Maybe it didn't... Who  knows. I half hoped a stray predator would have killed it."**

Lexi considers. "**Maybe when I split you, it not only split you from the Homunculus but also your Darker half...**" The thought has her standing with sudden anxiety. "**I only killed the beast, but your other half must have taken the research and the serum. There's no other way off this planet but our ship... But while he's here, he could be doing Force knows what kind of damage.**"

**"He will do everything in his power to corrupt the Temple. It's what I would do-- would have done. We've gotta go down there and cleanse the entire Temple of this Darkness... before it's too late."** Extending his hand, to help her stand, he adds, **"Sorry, but I guess morning tea is cancelled."**

Having dressed, armed themselves, and prepared appropriate gear, Wraith and Lexi soon find themselves once again in the room where the Jedi nearly died. Stairs led downwards into darkness, once Wraith had shown Lexi the newly discovered catacomb entrance. This one had been concealed underneath the bulk of the albino tree, which had disappeared from the Cupula, and saved Lexi’s life. Now, in its place, there remained a circular stairway leading downwards.
**"Huh, well that's definitely new."** Wraith points at  the strange black-blue unpleasant-looking slime trail that stretched deeper down the stairs into the Catacombs.
**"Better keep your eyes peeled for whatever left *that*."** Wraith ignites his new cyan blade, **"Allow me to go first."**

Descending the stairs, the duo exits into a large round room filled with sarcophagi, standing upright for rows upon rows upon rows, arranged in concentric circles. Each of the faceless humanoid-esque Sarcophagi has a name and title written in High Republic proudly inscribed across the chest. Several Sarcophagi are misshapen to look half-alien, the largest among these being several outstanding wookiees, as well as ithorians, and members of other alien species. The glistening blue/black slime trail winds randomly around the room, streaking across several sarcophagi and making tracking it's trajectory impossible. There is a passageway at the back of the room, leading further into the Temple. The center of the large burial complex contains the circular stairwell the duo had just descended from.

Lexi doesn’t argue with Wraith as he asserts the need to cleanse the Temple of this new and growing threat. It is with determination she dresses and goes over her equipment. Finding one of the concealed compartments, she leaves the book, still wrapped in her cloak, the tome and the holocron within and secures it. Before leaving she updates NZ on the situation and instructs the droid not to open the ship to anyone without the required passphrase, even if they had the appropriate rank plate. If force is attempted, she instructs him to fly the ship to a safer position. She fears that Wraith’s dark half would find a way past them and make for the ship, effectively leaving them stranded and unable to stop the Dark force let loose on the Galaxy.    

Now standing within the Cupula where the ancient albino tree once stood, she takes in the Chapel more thoroughly, as before she hadn’t the time to admire it. Idly, she scratches at the spot where Vaz’s lightsaber had pierced her. The memory of the small white twig sprouting from the spot comes to mind, but she pushes it to the back of her mind once more. The whole experience from the last day is something she decides is something to be shared with Master Gellendor.   

Her focus comes to the circular stairway and the ominous trail of dark slime leading downwards into the newly revealed Catacombs. She looks around to see if she can identify where the trail starts. “**A byproduct of a homunculus transformation?**” she suggests, before following Wraith down the stairwell, one hand on the wall beside her to keep her balance on the narrow steps, and another holding out a fusion lantern she’d brought from the ship to provide more light than the dim glow of a lightsaber.

At the bottom she makes a circuit around the room, looking over the nameplates of the faceless Jedi buried here, wondering absently if any were her long gone ancestors. She looks for signs any of the sarcophagi and the remains within have been disturbed by the slimy streak. “**It’s odd… On Tython we don’t bury our dead,**” her fingers lightly trace over a nameplate. “**We cremate them and spread the ashes. The crystals of their lightsaber are then inset into the wall of the Great Hall. Our names are kept in records, but there’s otherwise no indication of our lives or death.**”

As the duo strolls around the Central Catacomb Chamber, a sound echoes around the room. The sound of a cracking chunk of pottery, dull and making a distinct hollow popping sound, as one of the Sarcophagi begin to erupt from within. A mummified hand reaches outwardly, grasping an ancient partly wooden lightsaber handle. The handle ignites, with a yellow color. Slicing downwardly, the rest of the sarcophagus is bisected, and the Mummified High Republic Jedi Knight, steps out from the dust. His once-human skeletal grin is permanently plastered on his face, as he is still wearing golden/white robes embossed with the Stylized Star-like symbol of the High Republic. The noble knight, however, only has the outward appearance of nobility, whilst internally the Death Knight burns with a red, dark heart. It's eyes glow a menacing neon yellow-orange color, sickly orange in fact. The eyes flare and the yellow blade is instantly engulfed in hatred, shifting its hue to orange. It begins to waddle forward, un-naturally, as if testing its own ambulation abilities. As it picks up stride, it begins to run head on at the duo of explorers. The Creature leaps into the air, bringing down its glowing orange lightsaber above its head in an arc. Lexi and Wraith have only but a moment's time to react.

Lexi twirls expertly, managing at the last moment, as the orange blade comes down, to strike the mummy at the elbow joints, cutting off both its limbs. The orange saber deactivates, and flies across the room to clatter on the floor somewhere among the standing sarcophagi.  The disarmed Death Knight, seemingly unperturbed, continues its assault by chomping the air near Lexi's neck with its skeletal teeth, until Wraith finally puts the undead jedi out of its misery with a cross-wise cyan swipe across its torso, which collapses the mummy on the floor in a heap of dusty bones and burned charred nano-infused muscle tissue, that was only now beginning to unravel and turn into grey floating flecks, dissipating into the air.
Lexi looks in shock at the mummy as it disintegrates in front of them into dust. "**That .. that was unexpected.**"  Warily she looks at the other sarcophagi, fully expecting another mummy to emerge.
A hollow, urn-popping noise is heard in the distance somewhere, followed by a sound of pottery falling down and breaking elsewhere in the massive burial chamber.
Wraith stomps down on the corpse of the Death Knight, peaking up his ears, **"They must be attracted to our Force Signatures."**
Another 'pop' and 'crash' is heard from a different direction entirely.
Wraith points in the direction upwardly, **"We have to cut off our own entrance, otherwise these creatures are just going to climb up and disseminate into the jungle."**

"**Too bad there's no Ysalamiri around,**" she says quietly.  "**Have you have ever tried hiding your signature?**" 
At his suggestion she looks at the stairwell and slowly she nods. She tries to recall of there was any debris they could use to close up the passage that they'd be able to move later. Otherwise they risked trapping themselves... Permanently.
**"Have you got any explosives on your person, by any chance?"**
"**I do.. but I was hoping not to have to blow up our only obvious way out...**" She sighs as she pulls out the detonators she had brought with her.

In one far corner of the room, you hear the snapp-hiss of a lightsaber ignition, followed by the beginnings of a red hue, that is slowly getting closer. Along with the moans and groans and clicks of... something horrific and unnatural.
Lexi recalls that there was no convenient debris that she could place over the entrance to block it off, at the top of the stairs they had just descended.
**"We could, gain the stairs, and then detonate the stairs behind us once we're atop once more. Since you're the one wielding the detonators, its gotta be your choice."**

"**There could be another way out... And there's no time to find something up top to block the way.**" Grimly she sets the charge to the stairs and moves out of range of the impending explosion. With more mummies approaching she takes a deep calming breath and turns towards the closest noise.

A new Death Knight appears, stepping out of  the shadows. Its red saber gripped in one hand, its red eyes narrow and study the duo of explorers. Its gaze rests on the dead body of the previous Death Knight. Turning, it slashes one of the Sarcophagi, cutting its lid. The giant lid slams to the ground, revealing the curdled skeletal Jedi within. The red saber wielder places its free hand on the skeletal remains, and dips in between the Jedi's ribs. Suddenly, the eyes alight and the creature wiggles out of its coffin, igniting a blue double bladed saber, which changes to a red hue almost instantly. Both Death Knights now square off against Lexi and Wraith, twirling their red blades in alarmingly competent cooperation.

The two Death Knights attack in unison, with a coordinated flurry of lightsaber blows delivered by masters of saber combat. Both Lexi and Wraith are rocked back on their heels, pinwheeling as they can barely block or begin to register the volume and expert direction of each potentially lethal attack. Wraith's forearm gets grazed, leaving a nasty cauterized slash, while Lexi feels a hot poker stab at her shoulder, sizzling cooking flesh stinging her own nostrils.
"**They can corrupt others?**" she says in disbelief, suddenly feeling their course of action was no longer the smartest.

The duo of explorers begin to wonder if they can ever hope to match the skill and tenacity of the attackers. Both are convinced that no living creature could win in a straight up with with such capable combatants. Suddenly, Lexi's eyes glow a cyan color, and the world slows down for her to a fraction of an instant. Easily sliding out of the way of the next Death Knight's strike, she calmly twirls and cuts it in half, proceeding to disarm the second Death Knight in one smooth motion. The second Knight has its arm sliced in half, as its own saber drops to the floor. Wraith steps in and finishes it off with a confident forward swipe. Lexi's cyan glow extinguishes and the combat is suddenly over as quickly as it had begun.
Looking around, in the distance they spot a faint glimmer of light, coming from somewhere within the passageway at the end of the large burial chamber.
All around them, cracking, popping, and urn-breaking sounds are heard.
Wraith looks back and forth between the boobie-trapped stairwell and the far off glimmering doorway, **"Which way?"**
"**If it's all the dead... I don't think we'll able to handle them all... Let's retreat. Maybe there's another way.**"
**"Uh, sure, I'm all for getting out of here. You lead the way."**
**"I can cover our retreat."**
Lexi starts making her way as quickly as she can up the stairs before the revived mummies can catch up to them.

Although Wraith has to walk up the stairs with his lightsaber raised in guard position, he quickly retraces his steps. Soon, both Wraith and Lexi are once more at the top of the stairwell in the Giant Cupula where they had started. **"Blow it!"** Wraith dashes up the steps after Lexi.
Without a second thought she detonates the bomb.

There is a giant KRA-KOOM and the earth shakes. Dust billows up from below as both Lexi and Wraith are thrown off their feet. Rocks trickle down from the ceiling, then the rumble slowly halts and they can once more get to their feet, shaking dust off their pant legs.
"**It must have collapsed some of the tunnels,**" she comments, glad they had been above the explosion instead of below ground for it. "**Come on... I think it's time you met Master Gellendor.**"
**"Uh, what? Who's Master Gellendor? I'm so confused right now..."** Patting down his kilt, he coughs clearing the dust out of his way.

Dusting her own clothes off, she gives him a nod. "**Yes... I hadn't a chance to tell you yet... I found a Holocron in the Temple. The Guardian apparently is.. well... Apparently an ancestor of mine.**"
**"Holocron? Ancestor? Yours? Oh wow, that's... moving kinda fast... Hey, no offense, I don't think I'm ready to meet the in-laws just yet..."**
She shrugs. "**Suit yourself. I was going to see what he remembers of the catecombs.**"
**"Alright, well... How about you do that, I'll try to peek down and see what became of the room down below."** He sticks his head down the hole and looks around, coughing some more.
"**Just be careful... I'd rather not have to fish you out of the dark hole.**"
**"~Cough-cough~ Just a bunch of dust, we gotta wait till it clears."**
"**Let me know if something happens... I'm going to talk to Gellendor.**"
She turns away and makes her way towards the ship, giving NZ the passphrase to let her in.
**"Welcome aboard, Captain."**
**"I am registering seismic activity from the temple."**
**"Zero Wraith-look-alikes have attempted entry, so far. I am remaining vigilant as you requested."**
**"Greetings, young Student. I have thought much about what we discussed last, it seems I will have the honor of training one of the last remaining Jedi in the Galaxy. No pressure."** He beams.

"**I need your thoughts on a few things... Starting with the great tree that was growing in the Chapel.**" She goes on to explain her near death experience and how the tree disappeared.

**"Oh my, the Living Tree of Okhama, it finally became one with the Force... It's too bad, but I suppose all living things must come to an end one day. That is the natural cycle of life and rebirth, after all."**

content: "**It felt real enough. I could feel the energy it was absorbing into my body... But the sight of it.**" She looks at the Guardian. "**You might as well share. We we've already entered the realm of improbabilities.**"

Sighs, grumbling a bit to himself. **"I suppose that's true enough. Well, it's not an easy explanation. Have you ever heard of the Ithorian species, or of their homeworld of Ithor? Well, to make a very long story short, the Living Tree of Okhama, was the last of its kind on Ithor. It was in danger of becoming exinct at the hands of the Empire, and was therefore transplanted to Dantooine, where it would be better protected by our Order of Jedi Guardians, at the behest of our highest ranked Ithorian Jedi Master, Bo'kad Cril. The Guardians, along with the naturally defensive structure of this Temple, thought to help this rare species from going extinct altogether. For you see, no one knows how the Living Trees reproduce. They are fickle, powerful in the Force, and lend their aid only to a rare select group of individuals. It is felt to be, that their presence alone, is enough to sustain an entire living continent. They are some of the most potent life-sustaining plant species known in the Galaxy. Alas, if what you say is true, they are now, no more."** He slumps, saddening visibly.

"**That was the main reason I came here. We had to collapse the stairs that were under the tree to prevent them from coming to the surface. But we need to get down into the Catecombs. Is there another way in?**"

content:
Lexi lets out a breath of frustration, before putting the Holocron into a pouch. She grabs some rations from the galley before heading back to check on Wraith. Munching as she walks she considers what she learned, and what she didn't. 
Stepping next to Wraith, she casually hands him a small stack of the rations. "**How's it looking here?**" She glances over the edge of the hole to look down.
**"Messy. Apparently the blast has collapsed part of the ceiling, covering the entire Catacombs chamber with rubble, and crushing all the Sacophagi.... and seemingly all the undead Jedi alongside."**
"**Well.. I suppose we could repel down. Gellendor says there are other ways in, but there aren't any maps that would be able to lead us to them.**"
**"Hah. So much for relying on Jedi to help... It's always up to us, Sith, to roll up our sleeves, and do the hard work ourselves."**
He steps aside, showing the rapelling cable already snaking its way down to the Chamber.
**"What do you think I've been doing here, just laying around?"**

Lexi smiles at him. "**If only it wasn't a Sith causing all this trouble in the first place...**" she reminds him gently. "**I'll go first. You'll be able to pull me up faster then I would you if things take a turn.**"
**"Ladies first, then."**

Descending, Lexi enters the same Catacombs Chamber, however this time around the floor is littered with rocks, large and small. All the Sarcophagi have indeed been crushed, and only the edges peek out, once in a while, from clusters of massive stones that have burried the dead once more.
At one end of the room, the glittering doorway still sparkles in the distance.
"**Clear,**" Lexi calls up, giving the line a tug as she untangled herself from it. She waits for Wraith to join her before setting out for the door.
Sliding down, an unclipping the cable from his belt line, Wraith looks around and follows in Lexi's wake, igniting his saber just in case.
Crystals sparkled near the ceiling above the door, which depicted a tree, indented and carved into the stone.

With her own saber drawn and ignited, she uses the green blade to light her way towards the door. Her gaze follows the sparking crystals until they reach the door. "**The tree of Okhama...?**" Tentatively she reaches out to touch the depiction while looking for the mechanism to open the door.

Touching the tree depicted, a small white twig emerges from Lexi's slender wrist. It reaches, tentatively, to touch the depiction on the doorway. As soon as that happens, the tree embossed into the stone door, suddenly turns white and comes to life, in 2 dimensions, as its leaves also fill into their familiar, violet color. The twig quickly withdraws back into Lexi's wrist, but the colorful painting remains well lit.
The door, behinds to slowly grind open, lifting upwards to reveal a passageway further in.
**"Pffft... Show off."** He shrugs, smiling and striding forward.

Lexi blinks dazedly at her wrist and then at the door. "**Did ... you see that?**" she asks, wondering if Gellendor was right and she is going crazy.  "**That's the second time I've seen it.**" Her tone is hushed, almost in awe.
**"See what?"**
**"Your magic trick at the doorway? Yeah, I get it, you Jedi are all tree hugging hippies. Color me unimpressed."**
Lexi shakes her head, but decides not to explain. "**Nevermind.**"  Without another word she heads down the passage way.
The trail winds around for kilometers, outlined by a small worn path of rocks.

Around them, in every direction, the duo can see overgrowth covering every square centimeter of wall, column, and ceiling - only interrupted from above by high-up sky-lights, leading somewhere up towards the break in the jungle canopy.

Whistles, echoing. **"You Jedi sure know how to build 'em... So, what are we gonna do exactly, wander around until we run into more of Xod's lab experiments? Much as I enjoyed cutting down those Jedi Knights back there, somehow they don't exactly feel satisfying to kill."**
Lexi looks around in amazement at the unground jungle. Looking around further, she tries to find the trail of ooze left by whatever had corrupted the mummies in the antechamber.
There is no visible slime trail here.
"**Well, we can start by following the path. It has to lead somewhere. Maybe another exit.**"
**"Let's take a break here, seems as good a place as any. There's a nice friendly hill over there we can rest at. You're still hurt from the previous fight, why don't we treat your injury first?"**

Lexi has completely forgotten about the wound, having sustained so many various injuries over the week they'd been traveling. "**Alright. Maybe from there we can get a better view of the cavern.**" She follows Wraith to the top of the hill.

The faint drip-drip of some far-away cavern water cistern is heard echoing, as they sit on the wet moss that covers the stones in this region of the Jedi Temple, altogether resembling a typical hill. Stone walls overgrown with vines and artificial ponds dot the landscape.
The duo applies bacta bandages and Lexi's wounds are quickly restored. Sitting back, they simply catch their breath and regroup to figure out the next series of goals before them.

Lexi shifts her shoulder as she checks movement with the bacta patch. "**Thanks,**" she says with a small smile. "**This place is something... It seems to go on for kilometers.**" Having taken in everything with her eyes, she closes them and reaches out with the Force at the life around her and tries to pick out anything that doesn't seem to belong.
Lexi finds that there is nothing in her Force senses that registers as out of place here in this cavern.

Lexi opens her eyes, a frown creasing her face. "**I don't sense anything out of place... maybe it didn't make it in.**" Her gaze falls on the path they had taken and the door they came through. "**Maybe there's another way we didn't see.**"
**"The Sarcophagus Chamber was huge, it probably had other passages. This whole Mountain is one giant cavernous maze."**
**"To make matters more complicated, the explosion may have buried some of the passageways, including the one the Creature escaped down. Or, if we're lucky it got crushed in the collapse."**
**"Before we make our way too far down this particular passageway, we should first come up with a plan. This stony path has to lead somewhere, yes, but is that somewhere worthwhile traveling to?"**
**"I also suspect we may not have a choice, if the passages back there are all collapsed."**
"**Let's back track and check the Chamber first. We may find some clue as to where it went.**"
Lexi moves to stand, dusting off her pants as she does so. She can't, however, help stopping to gaze out into the cavern, the place holding a particular fascination.
As expected, the rest of the Sarcophagus Chamber appears to have collapsed, and buried any possible leads with it.
"**It's too bad we couldn't bring a speeder down here.  It would be helpful in covering ground.**"
**"Well, looks like we're in a bit of a bind. We could travel further down the passageway we started, but we could be getting further away from the Creature."**

**"Since the mountain appears to be a giant maze, we might stumble into an access area that could at one point, cross paths with the Creature somewhere deeper down in the Temple, but we would be relying on luck to pick up the trail we lost."**

**"Additionally, everythere here topographically slants downwardly from the Central Cupula. Therefore, if the Creature infects some of the local rivers with its Taint, then the *Entire Ecosystem* could become corrupted by Charybdis."**

Lexi smiles at Wraith coyly. "**We have the Force.**" Looking back into the passageway she considers. "**We can't go the way the creature did, but you're right these could be interconnected somewhere further in. It could be looking for the source, which could be the focal point of all the passages.**" Gazing up at Wraith, she frowns. "**It seems we have only the one path to take if we have any hope of success.**"
**"Alright, enough chatter, let's go and see where it leads."**
Nodding in agreement, Lexi sets out with Wraith in walking the stone path. She keeps an eye out for signs there might be side passageways, but otherwise sticks to the path.

The passageway winds down, taking multiple twists and turns, but along with the water trickling down through shallow ponds, the duo inevitably makes their way deeper and deeper. After 30 minutes of walking, the passageway finally opens up into a new spacious chamber - here yellow crystals within lamps stand and illuminate a grandiose chamber, at the center of which, the duo sees a wonderous garden, perfectly manicured and grown into beautiful cubes, perfectly square trees, other visually-pleasing geometrically shaped bushes, flowers of every kind, and all illuminated in a pleasant yellow glow. Benches are scattered around, but nobody seems to be here. The Force presence around this chamber is highly charged, and heavy in the Light.

The sight of the cavern literally takes Lexi's breath away. "**Amazing,**" she says with delight and starts walking further down the path. As they pass one of the beds, she reaches over to gently rub her fingers on the pettles of one of the flowers, almost in disbelief the perfect garden actually exists.
As she walks through the Garden, Lexi can feel different flickers and tastes of Light Signatures passing every which way.
Behind her, Lexi can hear a dainty feminine voice say, **"I beg your pardon miss."**
Lexi jumps up in surprise and turns towards the voice. "**I'm sorry...**" she starts.
Turning around, you see nothing but empty perfect garden.
**"Huh? What are you sorry for?"**
"**Uh.. Nothing. I thought I heard something.**" Looking around once more, she lets her senses in the Force track the different signatures. "**Let's keep going.**"

Lexi can feel the Light Signatures floating around her, then seemingly they lose interest and disperse. Meanwhile, she begins to become conscious of a Darker Signature emerging from one of the Garden Plants. This one appears to be a massive black rose with sharp thorns, veins pulsating with a blue iridescent throbbing energy.  The Dark Side Signature isn't just coming from the Corrupt Black Rose, but it is also more disembodied in the vicinity of the Rose, very close by.
The entire back half of the Garden, behind the rose is wilted and yellow.

"**Oh no... It's started.**" Lexi starts turning towards Wraith. "**Something is here corrupting the garden...**" With her saber drawn she casts a look about looking for the definitive source of the corruption.

Coming into view directly where Lexi is looking, a red translucent spectre appears disembodied and floating, echo in its otherworldly trembling feminine voice, bitter and hateful: **"Keep away from my beautiful black rose, Corporeal Ones."**
A hand raises, bearing extra long fingernails, curled and sharp looking. The hand slashes forward at Lexi.

The floating spectre swipes her sharp fingernail claws at Lexi's tender face. The Jedi attempts to block with her green lightsaber, but is surprised to find that the claw swipes straight through the blade and continues in its attack trajectory. Lexi has to do a nimble backflip to avoid getting grazed, nevertheless the swipe feels very Poisonously Dark in the Force as it flies past, just missing. The hood of the red translucent cloak of the scepter slips back down to its shoulders, revealing a hideously crazed looking human female, eyes bulging and her expression is a mask of hatred.
Interrupting briefly, Wraith attempts to hack the new arrival with the cyan blade, but his slashes seem to just pass without effect.
**"Alright crazy plant lady, back to your rocking chair--"**

The red spectre seems to refocus, eyes glowing red and hurls herself at Wraith in a flurry of swipes. Each swipe passes through Wraith's body, and although it doesn't make any visible gashes, you see Wraith's body jerk reflexively in response to the attacks, and a few seconds later, grey faint scars manifest on his skin where he would have been slashed.

Meanwhile, Lexi had made it all the way up to the Black Rose and cut through its trunk with her green lightsaber. Blue juices bubbled up, as the giant trunk fell, leaving behind a steaming stump bubbling up foul-smelling gasses and juices.
The Red Spectre howls and redirects its malevolent gaze at Lexi once more, flying in her direction.

Wraith summons his power in the Force, his Light Signature flares within his soul. A powerful invisible clasp fashions itself around the spectre's neck. It draws to a stop, halting mid air and struggling at its invisible noose, fingernail claws raking millimeters away from Lexi's face. However, it can draw no closer. Lexi hopped back in surprise at the viciousness of the assault.

His control in the Force slipping eventually, Wraith can't maintain the chokehold. The spectre breaks free, and Wraith is bowled over, rolling along the round. The spectre begins to dig her sharp nails into Lexi's torso with another vicious flurry of swipes. Long grey scars fade into being on Lexi's torso.
Fortunately the scars aren't deep, but they hurt in a special new way, like an ache deep in your teeth.

Lexi summons her Verdant Force Push ability directed with full power at the spectre, however, only to disastrous effect. The plants rise up at her command, but they pass right through the spectre. What's worse, they later turn grey and lifeless, falling flat and wilting. This seems to energize the spectre further, the red glow increasing. It goes on an attack frenzy once more, thankfully Lexi's plants shield her from this latest assault, but their life is forfeit in her place. Wraith attempts to rip the Spectre in half with his Force abilities, straining hard like popping a champaigne lid. The Spectre's leg rips off, but it seems not to notice and continues swiping away at the plants protecting Lexi.

By this time, a host of Light Signatures has gathered in a semicircle around the combat zone. Lexi summons all of the Force Power she has left, unleashing it all at once, successfully push with all her might, in response the spectre flies backwards with a Sonic Boom, tumbling end over end, and disappears into a rocky wall. After a few seconds, Lexi and Wraith realize they're alone once more. The Dark Signature of the red spectre receded very far away after the shove Lexi gave it, and now doesn't seem to be in her vicinity anymore.
**"--Huff-huff-- How long before that thing returns? --Huff-huff--"**

Lexi breathes heavily and her hand goes to the grey lines of scars created by the spectre. "**I.. I hope.. No time soon.**" With a pained groan, she moves towards where the black rose was and looks to see if the area was still being corrupted by it.
The area in the vicinity of the Black Rose remains ragged looking and evil, but the Rose itself seems to have been dealt a fatal blow, and disappeared into grey dust, as the last remnants float away.

As Lexi watches, the creeping yellow Wilting continues to advance very slowly through the garden, creeping one millimeter at a time. The Light Side signatures recede at the wave of yellow slowly encroaching.
**"Also, what the heck, my wounds aren't healing like they should..."**
**"Ouch. I've decided. I don't like feeling vulnerable like you Mortals. If we run into another one of those things, or five, we may not be able to make it out alive next time..."**

Lexi looks up at Wraith. "**The damage it did doesn't seem to have been physical... Nothing physically we could do could harm it... I've never heard of such a thing, but there it was. I wonder if the creature released it?**" Growing currently more concerned by the advancing corruption, she looks for the source it was emanating from. Perhaps if she found the source she could purify it like she had done the pool on the surface.

Looking at the back half of the chamber, she doesn't see any obvious source of corruption. In fact, inspecting it more closely, the yellow Wilting doesn't appear to be due to festering corruption, but instead due to a seeming lack of life energy.
Lexi goes back to the edge of where the wilting is. "**It's dying. I wonder if the Tree was giving this place life.**"
Slowly, she bends down and touches the dying plants with a frown.
Instantly, the dying plants she touches spring back to green verdant life.
Some of the Force Signatures floating around the room seem to levitate closer to Lexi in response as well.
**"Seems you've got a green thumb..."**

Emboldened by the change, Lexi starts to touch more of the plants intent on reversing as much of the progress as she can. "**I think... It's because the Tree merged it's life force with mine when it saved me.**"
As soon as Lexi touches one of the yellowed wilted plants, it returns back to life, however when she leaves behind any of the revived plants, they slowly begin to wilt yellow once more.
**"Is that why... you're playing tag with a garden of plants? Or is that just for fun..."**

Lexi stops and seeing that the plants begin to wilt as she leaves them, let's out a breath of frustration. Running about wasn't accomplishing anything. Instead she sits and focuses on the Force Signatures which she noticed started growing closer as she touched the plants.

As she does so, she closes her eyes, and under her eyelids can see about a dozen ghostly sentient outlines gliding around the garden. As she opens her eyes once more, the Force Ghosts are now semi-visible to her. The Chamber had been packed, it turned out, with a population of spirits. The spirits each wore Jedi robes. Some were alien, hammerheaded sentients with a long flute-like gently sloping head. They tended the plants with their ghostly arms. Many humans also flew around and enjoyed the garden, although some were gathering at the corner of the yellow Wilting, pointing at its spread with concerned expressions.
One of the ghosts is very close to Lexi, and is closely observing the reaction her touch is having on the wilting. The young female human spirit kneels over to inspect Lexi's work.
Wraith is being followed by a musclebound spirit, although Wraith is oblivious of this fact.

Lexi watches with amusement for a moment as Wraith paces about the garden with the spirit following him. "**Why don't you sit and rest? Are your wounds still not healing?**" Her gaze falls to where the spirit near her is inspecting her work. Tentatively, she reaches out to another wilting plant and brings it back to life. Her gaze doesn't leave the spirit.
The spirit seems to meet Lexi's gaze, **"Can-- can you see me?"**
**"I'll be fine, just stretching my legs. Place gives me the hibbie-jibbies."**
Ignoring Wraith now her attention fully on the spirit, Lexi nods. "**Yes... and the others. Why is the garden dying?**"

**"Oh thank the Force, you can *hear* me... Yes, please-- you have to help us. Yesterday, the Living Tree, it's essence vanished. Ever since that time... Our garden has begun wilting and there is nothing anyone can do to stop it. Soon, at this rate, the place will be nothing but barren rock..."**

content: Lexi sits for a moment not moving, as she looks at the dying garden. There was nothing that could be done, not in time to save vast gardens the Living Tree had kept alive for millennia.  Lexi shifts her gaze to Ten'elle. "**I'm sorry... I wish there's was more that I could do...**"

The frail ghost of a woman, simply shook her head. **"Thank you for trying, at least. In the absence of our Garden, all the Spirits will soon rejoin the Living Force. You cannot save our beautiful glaive, but we are already long dead - simply stubbornly hanging on to some green vestige of terrestrial life. Your act of absorbing the Living Tree will ultimately free us, to discover more possibilities out there... in the wider Galaxy."**

author.username: Ten'elle Shi'bar, Jedi Knight Force Ghost
author.global_name: Ten'elle Shi'bar, Jedi Knight Force Ghost

userName: Ten'elle Shi'bar, Jedi Knight Force Ghost

content: Slowly Lexi nods. "**Perhaps one day, the Tree will once again bring life to a new garden. Until then, I'll do my best to protect it.**" Standing, Lexi doesn't look behind her at the withering garden, instead inscribing the sight of the vast verdant garden to her memory.  "**Goodbye Ten'elle. It was nice meeting you, even for this brief time.**"
**"Fare thee well..."** She fades away.

author.global_name: Ten'elle Shi'bar, Jedi Knight Force Ghost

userName: Ten'elle Shi'bar, Jedi Knight Force Ghost

content: After saying her goodbyes, Lexi joins Wraith. "**Master Gellendor was able to clarify some things... One of which are the wounds the spectre inflicted on us... They can only be healed by the Force, specifically what he calls the Verdant Force.**" She points along the path they had been going which hopefully leads deeper into the mountain. "**He also told me there's a hanger deeper in the mountain. We need to get there before your darker self gets there.**"
****"I see, any idea how we could find it first?"****

"**He was unhelpful in that regard...**" She says thinking back. "**He mentioned sensing the Spiritual Flame or being an Ice Master... But he did say the maze would make it more difficult for him, but we'd have the advantage if we're patient.**"
**"Great... So... Typical Jedi rubbish..."**
Behind wraith, a spectre floats, watching your interaction.
Lexi gives an apologetic shrug and looks behind Wraith at the spectre. "**Do you know how we can get to the Central Vent?**" She asks wondering if any of the ghosts had more insight to the maze.

Seeing recognition in the eyes of the young Lexi, the spirit floats closer: **"I am Master Guardian Ken'ray Forus... and I know this Temple on a deep spiritual level. That is not the question, the only question is, are you worthy."**

author.username: Ken'ray Forus, Jedi Master Force Ghost
author.global_name: Ken'ray Forus, Jedi Master Force Ghost

userName: Ken'ray Forus, Jedi Master Force Ghost

content: His tone is deadly serious.

author.username: Ken'ray Forus, Jedi Master Force Ghost
author.global_name: Ken'ray Forus, Jedi Master Force Ghost

userName: Ken'ray Forus, Jedi Master Force Ghost

content: It made sense then why this particular spectre had been keeping a watchful eye on Wraith. At the question of their worthiness Lexi considers her response. "**Is our worthiness for the Force to decide... Or you?**"

Shaking his head slowly, he seems to take his time in answering: **"Do you ask such a question, when a Force Spirit is speaking to you, crossing the boundaries of Life and Death, amidst a Garden of Wonders woven by the Force itself? For all intents and purposes... the answer to your question, is an unequivocal *yes*."**

author.username: Ken'ray Forus, Jedi Master Force Ghost
author.global_name: Ken'ray Forus, Jedi Master Force Ghost

userName: Ken'ray Forus, Jedi Master Force Ghost

content: **"Hey um, who are you talking to now?"** He seems to look back and forth, squinting and twisting every which way.

"**Then let it be for the Force to decide,**" she says with equal seriousness. Her attention snaps back to Wraith as he asks his question. "**There's Spirits maintaining the Garden. One is a Guardian who knows the Temple, he'll help us if we're determined worthy.**" Cocking her head at Wraith she gives him a small smile.  "**If you calm your mind and open yourself to the Force, you might see them too.**"
Wraith rolls his eyes, **"Oh great, not you too with this Jedi mumbo jumbo..."**
Frowning, he adds: **"Wait... seriously?"**

Shutting his eyes closed, he balls up his fists and tries to send out his Force Signature into the aether, beginning to slowly begin picking up floating fluttering Force Signatures, but not able to spot Force Ghosts as yet, much as Lexi had to acclimate when she first arrived here in the Spirit Garden.
She raises an eyebrow at him as if to ask if she had ever led him wrong.
**"Well well, perhaps we may as yet witness... potential."**

Turning around, he springs at the appearance of the Guardian Force Ghost.

**"Oh-Oh-kay, so.... You weren't playing an elaborate prank this whole time on me? This place actually has Force Ghosts I haven't been seeing this whole time? How do we now they won't start attacking us like the other one did?"**
**"We would only attack you, if you had come here with evil aims."**

**"That Banshee was long ago once a part of our esteemed community, but unfortunately over time, she came to be corrupted by the Dark Side. She will not return, since her precious Black Rose has been at last... weeded."**

userName: Ken'ray Forus, Jedi Master Force Ghost

content: **"Oh-kaaaaay... So now what."**

**"You two are still inexperienced in the Ways of the Force, and more to the point... in the Ways of the *Light*. Only those who can see the Spiritual Truth with their own eyes, unhindered by Lies of the Sith, can ever truly be Worthy of inheriting the Mantle of the Jedi."**

author.username: Ken'ray Forus, Jedi Master Force Ghost

userName: Ken'ray Forus, Jedi Master Force Ghost

content: Raising one eyebrow, he looks at Lexi: **"Care to translate from Spirit-gobble-di-gook?"**

**"Let me spell it out for you then, Sith. This Temple you stand in now, was once the Greatest Learning Academy for Jedi in the whole Galaxy. It's sole function was to produce a Generation of Jedi to Protect the Galaxy from your evils - the Evils of the Sith. Here, those who become worthy, only do so once they have successfully accomplished their Ultimate Test and become True Jedi. Only a True Jedi can be deemed Worthy by the Temple, by the Planet, by the Living Tree of Okhama, and by the *Force itself.*" ** He had turned to Lexi, upon the pronouncement of that last phrase, **"Each of you here, now, stand at the precipice of Change. You can choose to either continue on your wayward quest for power, *or*.... You may let the Temple decide your fate. If so, then you would enter into your own personal Spirit Quest. This is a Quest you can only do in isolation, not as a pair. It will be an arduous ordeal. If you fail, you will die and Temple will have saved the Galaxy from another potentially disasterous Anakin Skywalker. If you succeed, you will be judged Worthy by the Temple, and by the Force. That, in short, is your translation, Sith."**

author.username: Ken'ray Forus, Jedi Master Force Ghost
author.global_name: Ken'ray Forus, Jedi Master Force Ghost

userName: Ken'ray Forus, Jedi Master Force Ghost

content: **"Hard pass."**

Lexi's mouth forms a grim line. She's not surprised when Wraith turns down the test and she doesn't look at him, instead keeping her gaze determinedly on the Guardian. "**What must be done?**" she asks evenly, there being no question in her mind her course.

**"Oh no... I'm afraid I'm going to need *both* of your assents, before revealing any more information to either of you. It's clear that you're here as a mated pair, and sharing information with one, would necessarily share it with the other. There is no room, in this Jedi Tradition, to allow for more Traitorous Sith to learn of our Deepest Jedi Secrets. You either both assent, or neither shall take The Test."**

author.global_name: Ken'ray Forus, Jedi Master Force Ghost

userName: Ken'ray Forus, Jedi Master Force Ghost

content: **"No thanks, I'm good on, uh... not dying in a spooky ghost-riddled Jedi ruin at the ass-crack of the galaxy, because a Ghost tricked me into voluntarily entering an eternal spiritual limbo world, where the rules are made up and the points don't matter."**
**"Let me guess old man, does this involve hallucinogens?"**
**"We Jedi do not need to cloud the minds of our followers. Everything you see, hear, and feel shall be real, I assure you. Only too real."**

content: **"Oooooh, that's reassuring then. Doesn't strike me as ominous at all. Get a load of this guy would'ya?"** Turns to Lexi, shaking his head.
**"Maybe we can stick 'im into a restaurant franchise on Corsucant, writing Fortune Cookies..."**

Lexi slowly turns to face Wraith, her expression neutral as she considers him. "**These sort of tests are ones of the mind.  We'd face what is already inside us and test our resolve for the course we've set.**" Slowly she asks the question: "**Are you afraid?**"
Groans, grumbling. **"No I'm not Afraid... but I'm not keen on voluntarily stepping into a trap."**
**"Besides... you've already seen what exactly comes 'out of my mind' as you put it. Would you voluntarily want to confront that once more?"**
**"This green light-bulb man says he can give us directions if he can hypnotize us into his delulu spirit Quest. You gonna take him at his word?"**
**"Never needed to go on any quest, Spirit or otherwise, just to find directions inside a structure the size of a Star Destroyer."**
**"Well... Maybe Super Star Destroyer."**
**"These names of vessels... do not instill confidence..."**

author.global_name: Ken'ray Forus, Jedi Master Force Ghost

"**I don't recall Star Destroyers being designed by Jedi with the sole intention of preventing people from just wandering in. We could wander the maze for the rest of our lives and still not be able to find out way.**"
Her expression softens as she looks at him. "**I've had to face your darker self before... You might find my darker aspects equally as terrifying.**"
**"Oh I neglected to mention, if you haven't figured it out yet, walking the same Pebble Path, will not always lead you to the same room, in the same way..."**

author.global_name: Ken'ray Forus, Jedi Master Force Ghost

userName: Ken'ray Forus, Jedi Master Force Ghost

content: **"Only the Temple will allow you to reach, whatever room your Spirit is Destined to step into."**

author.global_name: Ken'ray Forus, Jedi Master Force Ghost

content: Lexi looks at Forus. "**We're already in the maze... Even if we tried retracing our steps we wouldn't be able to go back the way we came... Could we?**"
**"You could... if you were Worthy."**

**"I say we just... Take our chances in the Maze to see where the Temple will deposit us next. If what he says is true, then it should lead us straight to the Dark One, right? If we're destined to stop him, in theory?"**
**"Potentially, yes. However... will you be ready to face your Darker Half, spiritually body and soul, in a final confrontation? Or will you be too scared and fail due to fear?"**

author.username: Ken'ray Forus, Jedi Master Force Ghost
author.global_name: Ken'ray Forus, Jedi Master Force Ghost

content: **"Rrrr... You seem to know a lot about me, Ghost Man. Well know this... *I'm not fucking scared of nothing*. Let's get your stupid Spirit Quest over and done with, so that we can all blow this popsicle stand."**
Nodding, he concedes a smile. **"Perhaps you are wise, in your own, naïve, way."**
