content: Lexi stands still for a long moment after the Spirit disappears and then turns to Wraith. She pauses as she gauges his frame of mind. "**He said it would be our own personal Quest. We won't be facing this together. Whatever, is to come... we'll be on our own.**"
**"Hey... what did he mean about it being obvious that we're a... mated pair, he called it?"**
Narrows his eyes, **"Alright fine... let's face our greatest fears, blah blah blah. I'd rather do that than stand around here yapping about Force Philosophy for 300 years..."**
"**Perhaps.. he could sense the Bond?**" she gives a small shrug.
**"More likely, I need to effect more of an air of hostility when we're out together in public. We're too easy to read, and our enemies could take advantage."**

She raises an eyebrow at him. "**I suppose... if that would make you feel better. Regardless... Take a moment to calm yourself. You don't want to go into this all worked up. It's going to be trying as it is.**"
**"Now that's... the first good suggestion I've heard all day, from a Jedi."**
**"Let's take the spotlight off me, now if you please. What exactly do you think *you'll* be facing against in this, so called, Spirit Quest?"**

Lexi stares at him for a long moment studying him before answering. "**I expect a variation of the Trials... Each academy has its own interpretations. This one... Seems very much akin to the Trial of Spirit, or as we unofficially call it... Facing the Mirror. It's one of the only trials that can't be controlled by the Masters since the trial is centered around facing the darkest aspects of oneself: fears, desires.**"
**"You damned Cheater, you're telling me you already passed this test back in Jedi Junior High? No wonder you weren't worried when Baldy suggested this whole crazy scheme to begin with."**
Pops in for a bored second:**"And by the way... My Students call me Master Ken."**

Waves at the area the ghost disappeared at: **"*His Students*...? No wonder you Jedi are all crazy. If you join each others' classes all day and take turns pretending to play teacher, then all your egoes are completely blown out from being overinflated with each of you running a Self-important Philosophy Class."**

Raising one eyebrow at the Face the Mirror phrase, Wraith rolled his eyes.**"Oh gooooood, well excuse me, if I took the Accelerated Sith Class, and we didn't have Magic Mirrors to practice against."**
With a small sigh, she puts a calming hand on his arm. "**It's a test of self; you are the Mirror... There's no practicing for this.**"

embeds.title: Berserk 2016 - Peering Into Darkness
embeds.description:
From the Berserk 2016 Anime Soundtrack
Composed by Shiro Sagisu

content: Your soul feels light, as you slump down, half asleep, but also half awake. Before you, you see something odd. Your own hunched shoulders and upper back, viewed from above and slightly behind. How can such a thing be?
Sudden wind sweeps you up, as you tumble pinwheeling into space, surrounded by twirling Galaxies and reversing sunsets.
The Scene fades away into mist, after which Lexi is left in a completely blank room, white and empty of all things. She walks barefoot on a white flat empty ground.

Fog banks enter the scene... and as they float past, Lexi once again finds herself transported, this time into a lush verdant forest. Ancient vines hang off ancient trees, grown atop ancient mountains. Mountains that have seen the Birth of the Jedi Order, and may as yet... witness its Death. The Mountains of Tython.
Lexi wanders, aimlessly, strolling with no particular direction in mind, as she finds herself carrying a bucket of water under one arm, as Dawn Breaks upon the Tython Village of Ardena.
Tython's Green Star burning away the Fog and Mist, Lexi walks through the central road of the Village, waving hello to passersby, all members of her Clan, the Ardena Bloodline.
One of Lexi's cousins, a girl of her same age, stops in front of Lexi, smiling broadly, **"Hi there Cousin, congratulations once more!"** She puts her hands together, clasped in joy.

Lexi stops suddenly as her cousin breaks her journey. She returns the smile, though it seems to escape Lexi what exactly she's being congratulated for. "**Thanks, Cousin...**" she says, if only to be polite as the other girl was obviously happy for her.
Her eyes narrow in concentration as she tries to remember her cousin's name and what she's being congratulated for.

The Girl smiles in response, nodding vigorously: **"Yes congratulations, you finally graduated and became a Jedi Knight, like you always wanted! I even heard you got the title of Chosen One, but gave it to Master Thrantir instead, just so you could come back to our Village and see us again. That was so selfless of you, Cousin. Absolute nobility: I'm so proud that the Ardena finally, too, have their own... Jedi Knight."**

The winds pick up suddenly, billowing both Lexi's and the Girl's hair. Above their heads, a whoosh can be heard, and felt, as wind buffets them both. Above their heads, floating just meters away, is a full Imperial Star Destroyer, grey and nondescript, standing on its nose end, pointing down at the Girl's head. The Girl smiles a happy smile at you, seemingly oblivious.
**"We are safe in your hands."**
Her flesh boils away in the flaring discharge of a green turbolaser, superheating into steam in front of Lexi's eyes.
The Vision drowns out in a green bright flare.
Once more she finds herself in a white, empty featureless room.

When Lexi sees the Star Destroyer, a pit forms in her belly. She tries uselessly to reach out to the girl in front of her, thinking somehow she could be shielded from what was about to happen. Instead her eyes open in horror before she's forced to shield them from the blinding green light. Her ears ring with the girl's last words.

Fading into existence, floating in the eether around her, several items begin to float around in a loose semicircular orbit around Lexi's center of mass. 1. Lexi's Khyber Crystal Necklace. 2. Lexi's Memento of Her Brother 3. Lexi's Smartsaber. 4. Xod's Syringe.  5. Wraith's Death Mask. 6. Wraith's Cyan Lifesaber, yes that's what its called. 7. Lexi's Rank Plate. 8. Lexi's Holocron. 9. Vaz' Access Card 10. A familiar-looking Junior Admiral's Rankplate. 11. N-Zee's Power staff, crackling black energy. 12. Breggor's Helm.
Lexi watches the items as they orbit her, nearly hypnotized by their movements. Were these objects representing aspects of herself and the people she had come to know?
13. White free floating baby tree, bearing purple leafs.
Lexi feels unsure of what to do for the first time, but somehow instinctively knows that touching an item will yield some... effect.

Lexi closes her eyes as she artifacts move about her, reaching out she grabs at the air in front of her until her hand catches something. Opening her eyes, she sees the junior admiral rank plate laying flat in her open palm.

White rolling billowing fog banks sweep in, blanketing everything in white momentarily, and then just as quickly, they roll away. Lexi finds herself on the command deck of a Star Destroyer. The windows are bright and clear, beautiful horizon stretching for miles ahead, sunshine streaming in. Happy white clouds run along skies of purest blue. Sitting diligently on top of the back of the Scarlet Command Couch, dressed head to toe in Scarlet Creaking Leather, sits Junior Admiral Mira Zircon, bearing the rank plate Lexi had just touched. She stares at Lexi in the eyes. No other people are visible in the room.
**"Welcome, dear girl."**

Lexi stands steady as her gaze first sweeps across the command deck and then finally their gazes meet. "**Mira...**" Lexi starts, but seeing the Reaper perched precariously on the back of her command chair changes her tact. "**I suppose here it's 'Admiral'...**"
Inclines her head, half-smiling, **"Naturally, that goes without saying... *Jedi Knight Lexi Ardena*."**
Lexi mirrors the movement in acknowledgement. Her gaze flickers momentarily to the windows, but then returns to the Reaper. "**Where are we?**"
**"Aboard the Crimson Justice. My Flagship. And you no longer have Pelleon's Skirt to hide underneath, *Captain*."**
**"I could have you drawn and quartered, right here, for my own amusement, if I so wished it. Do you doubt my word?"**
Lexi raises an eyebrow at the threat. "**I doubt drawing and quartering me would be the pinnacle of your amusement.  You have something else in mind.**"

**"Incorrect. Your Death is my highest amusement."** The scene changes instantly as Lexi feels her limbs torn apart by four chains that were wrapped around her limbs, while four Loader Droids tugged and split her in four pieces. The pain fades to red, then Lexi feels herself gasp awake, sitting on the floor of the previous Room, still bearing floating items - the Junior Rank Plate is gone, but a new item has been added: a worn Captain's Rank Plate. Othewise the other 12 items remain unchanged. Lexi takes [1d6] Spirit Damage.

Lexi hugs herself, the pain from the vision lingering as she finds herself sitting once more in the white room. At last she calms herself and is able to push the pain away. Retaining her feet she studies the artifacts once more. This time she reaches for the yellow kyber crystal.

Once again, billowing fog banks blow in, changing the scene. This time, you find yourself in the Tython Secret Mountain Temple, standing across the room from Jedi Master Davian Hux. Similarly, he stands away a few meters with his eyes directly looking into yours. Unlike the last time you saw this Temple, it is now Day-time and fresh mountain air circulates in this, the Central Chamber.
He has his Lightsabers Drawn, combat ready.
Artificial Right eye whirring, he slowly circles the room across from you, Lexi.
**"Best two out of three?"**

Lexi watches Davian circle her for a moment, her own lightsaber ignited and held at the ready. Then she's moving with him, keeping distance as they size the other up.  "**We came all the way up here to test our skill?**" she asks.
**"It was your idea, don't you remember? We should practice how the Ancients practiced, see what they saw... Breath the same air they once did."**
He strikes with his sabers, engaging in prolonged clash of multicolored lightsabers.
Parrying and blocking, he easily deflects all of Lexi's attacks, making her effortlessly look like a Youngling. Finally, both of his sabers point at Lexi's neck. He smiles, and backs off.
**"Maybe there's some merit to your idea..."**
For the first time, he seems unsure, pondering something seemingly to himself, as he stares at Lexi with an inscrutible gaze.

Lexi holds still until Davian withdraws his blade.. Now free to move without risk of decapitation she too withdraws. As he stares at her, she wipes the accumulating sweat off her brow. "**What is it? You look like you've eaten some of Master Brenca's 'special' pudding.**"
**"Huh? Oh it's nothing, lets resume our match."** He slowly advances and starts weaving more lightsaber strikes.

Effortlessly beating Lexi once again, seeing no improvement at all, he sighs: **"You seem distracted today, Lexi... Why not Sit and Meditate for a while yet, I shall return to Town. Think upon your Forms..."** He leaves.
Lexi is left alone in the Mountain Temple.
Once again finding herself beaten, she lets out her own sigh. Watching Davian's retreating form she starts to pace around the hall, the Central Chamber taking on a new life in the day light.
She recalls, the Jedi had a name for it: The Echineryon, or the Prophet's Monestary-Shrine. At the Central, she can discern where all the Ley Lines connect, there is a clear Focal Point.
This is the same exact area, where Lexi first stepped through the Portal.
Approaching the focal point, Lexi stands staring at the spot. Her foot gingerly traces the various ley lines as they intersect.
Lexi can feel the tingling thrum of power, almost an electric current, traveling up her leg.

Surprised, she jumps back at the touch of power. Wary she doesn't approach the focal point again. Instead she sits down nearby, her hand coming to rest on one of the lines as she opens herself to the Force.

Lexi's vision blurs and she winds up in the white blank room, once more. This time, the Khyber Crystal Necklace is nowhere to be found. Instead, a new item has added the floating parade of objects: Lexi's Holdout Blaster.

Picking up Lexi's Rank Plate, the fog once again changes the Scene. Our young Jedi finds herself transported onto a familiar scene, the Bridge of the Chimaera. There, Grand Admiral Thrawn sits in his Command Throne, regarding you cooly with his inscrutable alien gaze, eyes meeting yours, he is only a few strides away. Once again, apart from Thrawn, there are no other visible crew members or beings. The scene outside of the Star Destroyer is that of open Space, surrounded by a large armada of black-painted blunt-nosed cruisers, running lights blinking.
Lexi casts her gaze around before meeting Thrawn's gaze. "**Admiral...**" she says cautiously in greeting.
**"Ah, Captain Ardena. What brings you to my Bridge today, Captain."**
His tone seems perfectly civil and cordial.
**"You seem at a loss for words... You appear distraught. Why not tell me what's on your mind. As you can see, we're alone. Whatever it is that is bothering you, I promise, you can confide in me."**
"**I've been thinking about the mission. We don't know enough about their operations, the personnel.**"

His eyes glitter amusing at you, as if he knows what you're thinking. **"Come now, you can confide in me. I believe I've been your biggest benefactor thus far, haven't I? You still think I am actually in favor of restoring the True Empire?"**
Lexi cocks her head at him. "**I think you want to create an Empire... But with you at the helm. Trading one dictator for another.**"
"**While we're being so candid... I'm not convinced you'd be any better.**"
**"Correct. The Empire would be better served with me at its Helm. I don't intend to work this hard, just to hand over the reigns to a religious fanatic like Palpatine."**
**"As to your other assertion, I think you've already seen yourself that I treat my own subordinates with significantly better leadership. Being that you're one of them."**

**"Since we're being completely candid... here's the plain truth of the matter. You may not like my methods, but deep down, you know they maintain Order and Freedom. In this Cursed Galaxy, we don't have the option of a Mother Superior to lead the flock. One can pick only between different shades of tyranny. As long as there are beings in the Galaxy, they will always fight each other for limited resources. You can either pretend that fight doesn't exist, or..."** His red eyes flare. **"You can dive in headlong and *win*."**
**"In order to win, one individual cannot conquer a Galaxy. One must band together, and be united by an effective, logical, and intelligent leader."**
**"A *Grand* Admiral."**
"**A Grand Admiral who was given a title from a now dead tyrant. It gave you authority, but doesn't necessarily you worthy of it.**"
**"Death is the Fate of all Tyrants. I agree with you, that I am not worthy of the title I currently hold. However... Once I ascend... Then, I will be."**
Thin-lipped smile. **"Think of it more, as a Coming Attraction."**

"**And to ascend.. you'll enslave people to get what you want. Once you have the cloning facility it won't be another few years until you're able to put your plans into action. Do you really think you can hold out that long against the reigning Emporer?**"

**"Enslave my subordinates? Not at all. Are you a slave? Are any of my men slaves? No, they are here of their free will. If not, I don't want them in my service. The Clones will merely be... educated, appropriately. As for how long the Emperor's Reign shall last... Well, let's just say that all Empires fall one day, and by everyone's rekoning this one is in dire decline."**

"**Your education... is going to be no better than the indoctrination that was instilled on the Reapers... it will benefit you, yes, but you're removing their ability to make that free will choice. How will that not be slavery?**"

**"Tsk tsk tsk... How little faith you have in me, Captain. You wound me. I will merely instill in my future soldiers, the values of good moral virtue. The values of organization, order, military discipline, and overall - common sense. They will be taught the Classical Virtues of Justice, Truth, Reason, Beauty, and Morality. From there, it is simply for me to point out, that following my lead will lead to those ends becoming realized. If at any point, their goals do not align with mine, they will be free to leave and join Palpatine's failing Empire, if they so wished. However, I intend simply, to make a compelling case for my True Empire, over that of the Sith Empire. Do you see now, that I have no wish to lead Slaves?"**
Lexi raises an eyebrow at him. "**Words mean little without action put behind them. There's also the matter of the genetic material you'll need to create the Clones.**"
**"That's enough chatter Captain. Tell me why you're here. I don't imagine you came here to debate free will versus predestination."**
"**The time is nearly upon us, Admiral... I'll keep to my word and not interfere with your plans.**" Keeping Thrawn's gaze she adds evenly: "**That doesn't mean I won't be watching.**"

**"It's not the answer I wanted, but it will do. I can't turn you into a True Believer, but you can still serve as my Ally. Fare well, and what is it that you Jedi always say... May the Force, Be with You."**
The mists roll in, and deposit Lexi back into the blank white room.
Notably, there are only 12 items floating now.

Selecting the floating Syringe, the mists once again transport Lexi to a new location. This time, she finds herself on the top floor of the Medical Institute. Here, Clone Xod, stands looking out at the Plaza of Ambulances below, hands folded behind his back. Turning, he makes eye contact. You are sitting in his Personal Office. It is prim, proper, and pristine. Friendly Health Holoposters cover the walls. You suddenly realize, this is the same room you were strapped into a gurney to, except now it has been reverted to its previous, medical status. Xod looks at you with his purple eyes twinkling, wearing a full White Coat and carrying a wide array of nasty surgical implements sticking out of every pocket. Although the Scene is serene, once again, the room is empty of all other beings. Sitting in the Visitor's Chair you look into Xod's eyes. Clearly, although admist a peaceful room, you can still see the madness dance in the Doctor's Eyes.
**"Good morning. I'm Doctor Xod. So... What seems to be the problem?"**
Outside, normal hospital business is carrying on as usual.
A child, with its head deprived of hair, is in a wheelchair and being rolled forward by a nurse. A group of paramedics carry a gurney with a sick alien.

Lexi is momentarily taken aback by the clash of opposites. She'd only seen Xod fully in his madness, but here, now, was a version that had his sanity enough to continue to help people. "**I've heard about the work you've been doing, Doctor. I'm quite in awe of what you've managed to accomplish.**"

**"Truly?"** His face seems to lighten, wrinkles smoothing away, **"Yes, one day I shall create the Perfect Serum, that will heal all disease, and grant everyone immortality. Imagine it... No more pain, no more children dying of disease, no more..."** He smiles, one corner madly twitching, **"Weakness."**
**"Then again... I never expected you, to understand. You were merely a stepping stone, to the completion of my Dark Dream. Is the result not marvelous to you, Jedi?"**
"**Your perfect serum... has reduced sentience into barbarity,**" she says truthfully. "**Surely there was a way to accomplish your goals that didn't resort to turning people into... monsters?**"

Shakes his head slowly, in frustration, **"No no, my intent was never to manufacture Monsters. Those were merely Intermediate Stages, that also served me as Lab Assistants and Personal Guards. You never read my research yourself did you? If you had, you would understand..."**
**"Then again, maybe you *would*, given that you were finally able to complete my research... with your own two hands."**
"**Explain? How did I complete your research?**"

**"For all of my considerable scientific aptitude, for all of my genius in molding and shaping the genetic strands of Life Itself, I was completely powerless to shape the Soul of my subjects. You, on the other hand... You alone, have the Genetic Bloodline Power to cultivate The Perfect Dark Soul... and combine it with my Perfect Serum, completed by my wayward Apprentice."**
"**You're saying by splitting them.. I completed what you were ultimately trying to accomplish all along... to create a being fully immersed in the Dark Side?**"

**"I aimed to create the Perfect Sith Homunculus. The Light in the Soul of my Test Subjects, was perpetually at war with that ultimate goal. By performing a, shall we call it, conscience-ectomy, you've finally completed my work. The Creature that wanders these Halls, is the result of all of my life's work. It has the powers of every Bloodline, and has no limits of Conscience to prevent its gifts from being utilized."**
"**How did you hope to control this perfect being of yours?**"
**"My plan was to install a killswitch, in case the experiment went... awry."**

"**A killswitch?**" For some reason she finds the thought amusing. "**It seems even you weren't confident you'd be able to control your creation.  You had to have thought up a counter in the event the killswitch wouldn't work. An anti-serum, perhaps?**"

**"No, nothing so dramatic. I merely built in an inherent limitation into Project Charbdys. The Serum utilizes a rare element called Hexathite. This element is metabolized at a high rate, and I control its only supply on Hyperion. If an experimental subject were to... stray, to be loose, and go rogue on my planet, it would always by necessity die, due to a rapid consumption of Hexathite. It was a killswitch in case of catastrophic failure. That was why I broadcast the 500 kilometer no-fly zone."**
Lexi frowns. "**How rare are we talking? I've never heard of such an element. And how quickly would it metabolize? **"

**"Hyperion had a supply, thanks to Czerka Corporation's Mining activities, I was able to refine it and reshape it for my own purposes. Very few bodies around the Galaxy contain Hexathite, perhaps less than a dozen. It should have all burned off after 24 hours. However... Wraith's DNA must have somehow found a way around this limitation. His Regenerative Bloodline must have found an alternative compound to serve in place of Hexathite. I theorize it to be an organic compound of some type, that slots in to serve the same puporse as Hexathite. I'm afraid from there, it's all conjecture."**
"**Is it something he could pass on to other Homunculi?**"
**"In theory, yes, there is nothing to stop him from sharing this gift with his Children."**
"**Children as in those he infects with the serum?**"
**"The perfect life form, would have to reproduce, in order to survive, would it not? Otherwise, it isn't the perfect life form."**
She restrains a shiver. "**Any children he would have... wouldn't be a perfect copy. They'd have complete souls. Not split like his.**"

**"I'm afraid, that my expertise, does not extend into the realm of... Souls, as you refer to them."** He looks at his watch, **"I'm afraid our time together is almost at an end. I have appointments to keep, lives to save. If you'll excuse me?"**
Raises one arm, palm upwardly facing, towards the door.
Lexi stands, preparing to leave as Xod requests but then stops. "**I have one last question: Reaper age acceleration... Does it get flipped off?**"
**"You'll have to elaborate."**

"**The Clones created for the Old Republic aged at a rate of twice that of a non modified Human, but the gene modification was never reversed or designed to be switched off. Going off the gestation rate of a Reaper child, I presume the rate of maturation continues when the child is born until a certain point. Or do the Reapers continue to age at nearly 4 or even 5 times that of a their species?**"
**"I understand now, what you're referring to. Yes. After reaching the age of 22, their maturation is halted into a near-perpetual adult stage."**
**"My patients are waiting, I must ask you to leave now."**
"**Of course... It was...**" she pauses lacking a better word finshes: "**Enlightening.**" Without much further preamble, she heads out of the pristine office.
Exiting from his office, Lexi steps once more into the White Blank Room of Floating Mementos.
She ponders her choices before picking her next object. There are only 11 remaining.

Now once again in the white room, Lexi studies the remaining objects then closing her eyes once again reaches out to grasp the next object. Her fingers wrap the familiar grip of her lightsaber and she doesn't have to open her eyes to confirm.
**"Welcome welcome, Young Jedi. Make yourself at home."** Lexi finds herself inside the small, squat cavern that comprises Master Xoda's workshop beneath the Academy on Tython.
**"How are you liking your new and improved smartsaber?"**

Lexi looks about the cavern and the cautiously picks up some random part from the workbench. "**For having such a short time with it, Master. You managed to improve it with a great many features.   I've found the recorder quite useful.**"

**"I'm certain you have, Child. I just don't think you've come to fully appreciate, the mechanical aspect of the Force. Not every solution can be found with mysticism and diplomacy. Some answers can only be found by Creative Construction. Care to try your hand at constructing something?"**

Lexi looks about at the various bits and bobs.  "**I've been meaning to construct a second saber,**" she says putting down the part she had picked up and choosing another. "**Master Davian gave me a kyber crystal before I left...**" she lets the thought hang in the air. "**Do you know where he got it from? It was resonating with other crystals on Dantooine so I've suspected it originally came from there.**"

**"I'm afraid I just don't know, the resonance could simply be an energy interaction between 2 Force-charged capacitors. The crystal could have originated from anywhere. As for your idea of constructing a second lightsaber, I believe that would be acceptable. If you pass this test, you will obtain a new saber, but if you should fail, the crystal may be forever broken. Do you still wish to try?"**
**"[Just don't roll a Nat 1.]"**
Lexi pauses for only a moment before setting the part down and turning to look at Xoda. "**Yes.**"
**"Here are enough materials to get your task finished. I must take my leave. Good luck and... May the Force be with You."** He disappears into one of the multiple passageways in the cavern.

Lexi gets to work, pulling the necessary parts from the ample supply Xoda had ready for just such a task. Completing the handle and then an emitter she suddenly found herself having difficulty putting them together. Connecting the fusion core resulted in a short in the wiring and all her work bursting into flames. Caught in the blast she scrambles to put out the fire before it can spread. 
Gingerly she touches the new burns on her forearm. With a sigh she looks around. Seeing there were enough parts to attempt it again, she goes about it. The handle comes easily enough, but this time the emitter gives her difficulty. Stopping she quiets her mind and her hands move automatically to complete the emitter. She hesitates as she comes once again to the fusion core. After a few false starts, she finally is able to connect the core to the hilt.
The moment of truth at least has come as she pulls loose the crystal from the cord. Holding it lightly in the palm of her hand, she frowns at it. With a nudge of the Force she focuses on it and puts it into place below the emitter. Closing the chassis, she lets out a breath. 
Standing, she activates the blade and marvels as the yellow blade shots up, casting it's radiant glow about the cavern.
The white puffy banks of fog gradually roll in from the periphery, and as the flare of the yellow blade fades, Lexi again finds herself standing in the blank white room. 10 floating objects remain.
The ten objects make their orbit around her and once again, closing her eyes she reaches out. Her fingers brush and latch onto Breggor Kryze's helm.

The fog once again transport Lexi, this time to a sandy fighting circle, inside a cavern, ringed by flaming torches. A massive Mythosaur Skull hangs on one wall. Sitting with legs crossed ahead of her, is a Mandalorian wearing the Helm.
**"So you've come."**

Lexi sees Kryze sitting at the center of the flaming circle. Slowly she approaches. "**Would you rather I hadn't?**" Stopping a few paces away, she takes her own seat and mirroring his position, sits cross legged. "**How things ended...is not what I wanted.**"

**"This is an ancient Arena of my People. Since my arrival here, I have come more and more, to appreciate the Mandalorian culture that I left behind, long ago. I don't know how long I've been here, but I somehow, instinctively knew, that you would arrive." He stands, "I demand a rematch. Only through honorable battle shall I redeem myself. Take up your weapons. If you best me, I shall grant you One Boon of your choosing. If I best you, then I shall be redeemed in the eyes of my Ancestors."**
Lexi stands as he does and does she's bidden in drawing her lightsaber.l, the yellow blade coming to life. "**If this is what you want...**" On gaurd, she prepares herself.
Breggor draws twin Beskar Vibroblades, pinwheeling them expertly.

A flurry of slashes assault Lexi, who expertly utilizes footwork to avoid most of them, letting one nasty vibroblade slip past and draw a deep gash on her tummy. Counterattacking, Lexi attack with both her green and new yellow blade, finding her offensive power greatly boosted. Breggor's suit is glowing red with a single long lightsaber swipe, and sparking with electrical short circuits. Twirling around, Lexi delivers a series of twin slashes and kicks Breggor into his solar plexus. He goes down, landing on his back, chest smoking with cooling fusion slices, as both Vibroblades drop to the sand.
A Mandalorian Smith appears behind Lexi. Her voice is feminine: **"Well done... You may... select your prize."**

There is a small rock table with three objects placed atop. **"You may choose to Drink the Living Waters [+1 UtF permanently], to don the Mythosaur Bracer [Beskar material, built-in Needle Rocket], or Eat the Heart of the Basilisk [+10 HP permanently]"**

Lexi whirls, breathing heavily as a new arrival comes up suddenly behind her. Quickly realizing the threat has passed, she turns off the blades and attaches them to her belt once more. Drawing closer to the tables, she looks upon the objects. Finally, she picks up the heart and frowns wondering if she was really going to have to eat it.
"**What will become of Breggor?**" she asks, the Smith.
The Smith has already disappeared.
So have the other two objects.
Breggor's body is gone too.
The Heart is thick and meaty.
It fits barely in one hand, and radiates endurance.
Lexi estimates it to weight about 10 lbs.

Now alone in the fiery circle, Lexi looks down at the heart in her hands. "**Well... Only thing left..**" Hesitating for only a moment she takes her first bite of the dense piece of flesh and continues until it's all gone.
After one bite, she closes her eyes against the sour taste, as she reopens her eyes she has returned back to the White Room.
Lexi feels a new power in her limbs, where there was weakness before, she looks down to see that her muscles and abs have become toned.
9 Objects remain

Selecting the Death Mask, Lexi finds herself transported by billowing mists to a strange dimension of Escher-like never-ending stairs of all shapes and colors. Across from her, standing upside down, is the familiar shape of Dark Wraith, wearing his Death Mask.

Lexi is filled with a sudden dread as her hand finds the familiar shape of the meask suspended in the air. As her eyes open she finds him standing before her, though upside down, the mask firmly in place. The strageness of the place takes her gaze away from him, as her mind attempts to orient itself to the fantastical scape.
**"~Mother~"**

Her attention snaps back to Wraith and she resists a shiver at the term. "**Is that how you think of me? I suppose in a way not far from the truth... I created you from the darkness that was inside Wraith's soul.**"
**"~Yessss~... ~Fertile, Beautiful, Strong in the Force~... ~Welcome Home, Mother~."**
"**'Home' could use a decorator,**" she says dryly.
**"~Mother Will Stay Here Forever, Decorator~ ... ~Yesssss~"**
Ignites a Double Bladed red saber
"**I think not...**" She responds by activating both of her own blades and prepares herself.

The Dark Wraith Creature draws its arm back, and tosses the double-bladed saber directly at Lexi. The blade spins along its fulcrum, like a bright crimson helicopter. Lexi first tries to deflect with both of her blades, but realizes the futility immediately as the red blade seems to be controlled telekinetically, and can come in at such an angle as to make it impossible for her sabers to reach it due to simple human limitations. Trying her best, she only manages to redirect it before it flies by her shoulder, leaving a painful cauterized gash. Lexi, meanwhile, has bulit up her Force energy and releases it all at once, a gust of air slamming into Dark Wraith, as he goes tumbling into the zero-G space between stairs. Pinwheeling, he manages to land somehow, unsteadily on a further stairwell somewhere deeper in the endless stair dimension. Unfortunately, entities flying in this dimension find themselves buoyed, and therefore, take no damage.

Clasping at the wound, Lexi gazes down over the edge looking for where Wraith had gone in the topsey turvey maze. With a frown she turns to look at the series of stairs and doors her own platform led to. With a shrug, she makes a decision and kneels in her spot, content for the moment to wait and see what the Dark One would do. She draws in the calm and waits.

The Creature, catching its blade, as it had lost concentration, stood once more. It was still upside down from Lexi's vantage, but its red glowing eyes were studying her intently. Walking calmly to one of the doors, it opens and steps through.
On either side of Lexi, she sees two doors open and 2 Dark Wraiths step through, each wielding a single red blade.
Wraith 1 running down stairs, while Wraith 2 runs up the stairs, both charing at Lexi.
Meeting the attacker, Wraith 1 with her frontal defense, Lexi's leave her back turned to Wraith 2, letting him slice her back, forming a black cautery gash.
**"~Mother~... ~We are your Children... Why do you hate us so...~"**
Wraiths halt, having Lexi surrounded and damaged. They turn their heads at 45 degrees looking at you with two different metal Death masks.

"**I don't.. hate you,**" she pants, her back aching from where one of their lightsabers had hit her. "**You were always part of Wraith... and I accepted that. It's what you have the potential to do... the serum. The serum should be destroyed so it can't be used again.**"

The Death Masks seem to twirl, becoming miniature black holes, as they suck in their bodies. All that's left of the 2 Wraiths are 2 Floating Death Masks. They begin to circle Lexi. One is happy, one has a sad expression. They don't seem to speak, but slow down eventually and both float in front of Lexi, slightly bobbing up and down. Then they fuse into One Death Mask, black with red glowing energy lines. It starts taking, in a deep demonic voice: **"~I shall conquer all, Mother. You'll be proud of me. Just watch me~..."** The black hole reverses and a new Wraith appears standing attached the full mask.
**"~I shall Conquer the Galaxy. You and Father and Brother shall be proud.~"**

The creature closes down its red blade. **"~I shall kill all your enemies. Palpatine and Jade. All of them. I can do it, you know. I'm stronger, faster, immortal. My Mother and Father made me that way. Thank you...~"**
Lexi is taken aback as the homunculus backs down, her own blades still held at her sides. "**At what cost? What cost of innocents?**"
**"~The cost doesn't matter, all that matter is that you're happy, Mother~"**
"**If you think that would make me happy, you don't know me at all.**"

**"~I think I know my own Mother... You're bashful, I understand... Don't worry, I won't tell anyone... You too want to Overturn the World... I will do it all For You, Mother. Just wait... you'll see...~"** His body begins to bulge, as one shoulder muscle suddenly swells to twice its normal size, bubbling like an unstable tumor. The shoulder explodes, as Wraith seems not to notice, and quickly regenerates, healing once more. **"I can't maintain this Form long... Please go now, Mother... I promise I'll travel to Coruscant and Annihilate the Emperor Reborn. Then, we can be a Family Again. Fare well.."** He turns and starts walking toward one of the doors.

Lexi jumps back in alarm as his shoulder bulges and then explodes, his form showing evidence of instability from the transformation. Whatever he was using to stabilize the transformation wasn’t holding and was using his healing abilities to offset the deficit. “**If only that’s where it would stop… How many will die in that pursuit that don’t need to? And what then afterwards?**” She shakes her head knowing that as he walks towards the door to leave that she may not get her answer. It’s then too she realizes her goals had been perhaps too narrow. 
Intent on her mission to stop the Clone from going into the past, she assumed once she did that, her journey would be at an end. But what if it wasn’t. There was no telling if time would reset, or if like a branch on a tree, the reality she is in would continue and grow. This time and reality is now hers to find a place in, unless she finds a way back. Which means the course of who would fill the power vacuum left by the Emperor Reborn and would be able to affect the course of the Galaxy going forward is in question. Would it be Thrawn… or someone else?
One thing is for certain: it isn’t going to be another Sith.
Lexi returns to the Blank White Room, now only 8 Objects remaining in orbit.

Lexi immediately realizes she has to make some critical assignments of her troops right away, as they are being cut to ribbons. Davian's Squadron is still a few seconds away, approaching to flank Lexi’s Battle Group. 

Lexi orders the Gauntlets and the Defenders to battle against the dragons. She orders the last remaining X wings to guard the rear for the time being, knowing her Coralskipper Vanguard will get the brunt of Turbolaser disintegrations and Dragon fury. Over the telepathic link, she orders her troops: **“Torpedoes, lock onto those three Flying Beasts: single barrage, full spread… Fire!”** After a brief delay, 50 missiles fly forth from the ships comprising Lexi’s Assault Force, heading steadily for their violently thrashing titanic targets.

Each Mutant Dragon fights furiously with 3 bites in quick succession, 2 wing buffets, and 1 tail swipe. Each attack is titanically powerful, the Charybdis Beasts bearing powerful vat-grown musculature. The Coralskippers, despite receiving Calming Telepathy from their Yoric Vec, are caught by surprise and nearly wiped out. 10 out of 24 Coral skippers are crushed, bitten in half, swatted away, or otherwise pulverized. However, the 2 squadrons put up a valiant fight, scoring the first few crucial hits on the Dark Side Dragons, leaving a few small wounds, but not nearly enough to bring them down. Fortunately, the Coralskippers keep their 3 massive opponents occupied, as Lexi’s 50 missile-spread slams into the feral beasts. One of the dragons, the smallest of the three bearing Magenta nano-polymer skin, is vaporized in a massive explosion, bloody chunks raining down. The remaining dragons, black and violet in hue, are critically injured as they catch a whole swarm of torpedoes. The Black Dragon is mortally wounded, but continues thrashing its limbs at the buzzing cloud of Corralskippers in its vicinity. The Violet Dragon, bearing Xod atop its neck, becomes severely wounded as a result of multiple Torpedo detonations, but manages to remain in flight, retreating behind artificial cloud cover. As Xod dives down and away into the red clouds, the feral mortally-wounded Black Dragon engages with the Corvettes and TIE Defenders, who squarely finish it off, but in its death frenzy the Dragon manages to take down 1 additional TIE Defender.

Davian and the ARC 170s are now in missile range, but haven’t yet closed to Dogfight range. The 14 surviving Coralskippers have finally made it to the *Crimson Justice*, and begin focusing their Doval Basals to bring down the ISD's shields next. Admiral Zircon’s flagship fires its Turbolasers, as the ISD attempts to vaporize the Coralskipper Vanguard. 2 Coralskippers are evaporated out of existence, but the remaining dozen ships survive past the withering hail of turbolaser fire. Engaging grav-beams, 12 micro black holes spool up using advanced organic alien technology. In response, the majority of the shields seem to be sucked away into a dozen microscopic pinpoints of gravity created by the Coralskipper starfighters, leaving only a small fragment of energy protecting the hull of the ISD. 

Lexi calculates that her anti-capital ship torpedoes must be fired in 2 successive waves. If all of them are fired at once, all they will do is bring the Shield to zero, popping it, but not hit the hull at all. The firing of missiles at an ISD is a very precise affair. The barrage is split generally speaking, into 2 staggered waves, mere seconds apart. The first round of missiles bursts the shields, the second penetrates into the hull before the shields can recharge. Out of 132 possible total missiles fireable in one Assault Run, she assigns a fraction into the first wave and remainder into the second wave, telepathically yelling out her orders into the Comms Beetle: **“Aim 42 Torpedoes at the ISD, first wave… Fire!… Remaining ships… Aim 90 Torpedoes at the ISD, second wave… Fire!”**

Out of 42 missiles, most are intercepted by Point defense lasers, but 18 missiles make it through to successfully impact the shields. The blue magnetic shield bubble pops, as the shield generators overload and auto-shut down to prevent themselves from detonating. A fraction of a second later, the second wave of 90 missiles flies at the exposed hull of the *Crimson Justice*. Although 36 of the torpedoes are vaporized out of the air with accurate shots from the Point Defense Lasers, the remaining 54 missiles impact the hull in a series of brilliant detonations. The entire length of the ISD is strafed with a line of explosions, one after another after another. The red painted capital ship, seconds later, is smoking from multiple fires along its central length. Missiles detonate inside the ventral Landing Bay, transforming it into a charred blackened gaping hole. The Landing bay is critically damaged, its environmental bubble having been popped, air is leaking out of the Bay, alarms blare as bulkheads attempt to seal shut. Meanwhile, several torpedoes had passed the *Crimson Justice* and now vector 180 degrees to strike the ISD from behind, detonating into the massive engine funnels of the ISD, secondary explosions rendering the capital ship’s massive hyperdrive inoperable. Despite all of this, the *Crimson Justice* remains afloat, despite being a flaming smoking raw wounded majesty of Imperial design. After the bombing run is complete, the twin shield generators begin to re-engage, beginning to regenerate the magnetic shield bubble around itself. Lexi realizes she has dealt a severe blow, but the gigantic ship is not finished yet.

A trio of attacking groups break out of the smoke clouds beneath the ISD, rising into view. The central ship, plainly visible, is a smaller Imperial ship, painted matte black - the Raider Corvette. Flocking around it are, what look to be, a couple dozen starfighter-sized Flying Homunculi, each an unspeakable horror in its own right: deformed, sporting odd numbers of limbs, and rows upon rows of uncontrollable growths of Charybdis spikes. The sheer biomass protects Dark Wraith’s ship, safely at the rear of the formation, as the Flock screams and caws and screeches every evil tentacle, wing, tail, and limb at Lexi’s Battle Group. To make matters worse, on either side of the Raider Corvette, are two more Flocks of Homunculi, each supported by a Crimson Imperial Lambda Shuttle. Now Lexi must face down 4 Separate fast-moving battle groups, while the ISD remains hovering slowly in place as it regenerates its capital-ship-class shields slowly, but steadily.

Lexi gives orders for the Coralskippers to take cover by staying against the hull of the ISD and continuing to diffuse the regenerating ISD shields. Ten micro-black holes activate in response, sucking up all the shields like a sponge taking up water. Meanwhile, the Gauntlets deliver a full volley of Proton Torpedoes at the *Crimson Justice*, sparking a dozen blue detonations across its dagger-shaped hull. One of the dome-shaped Shield Generators catches a Proton Torpedo directly and explodes. The embattled *Crimson Justice* shudders, but remains afloat. 

The young Jedi gazes across the bridge of the Yoric Vec. Closing her eyes, she mentally directs telepathic waves of calmness through Okhama, and feels the minds of the pilots of the TIE Defenders as they swirl around her organic flagship. ** “Our victory is close at hand, the enemy have revealed their final chess piece… Longswords, Broadswords… Bring down that Raider Corvette!” **

The 2 Squadrons of TIE Defenders, depleted down to 17 from their full strength of 24 engage their drives as they ignite to life, streaming in at the Raider and its flock of Charybdis Mutants. Quad laser cannons open up with green blasts, leaving puffs of blue in the air as they vaporize the organic abominations. The furious assault takes a toll, as 3 TIE Defenders are ripped apart by the flying mutations before Lexi’s forces destroy them. The Dagger shaped Raider Corvette sits in the open at last, as Lexi’s Longswords and Broadswords clear Dark Wraith’s vessel of cover. 

As if in answer, it fires a perfectly aimed Turbolaser at one of the TIE Defenders and it explodes. Lexi can feel the telepathic anguish of the pilot’s soul, extinguishing. Two more Turoblasers from the Corvette strike Lexi’s Yoric Vec Cruiser, and she feels the ship shudder. Oki cries out in pain in response, white blood-sap streaming down her body like milk.

Gripping the arms of her chair, Lexi orders Oki, **“Volcano Cannons, Fire!!!”** After 5 thunks audible and palpable in Lexi’s chest from the Yoric Vec’s capital ship weaponry, the Yoric Vec’s Volcano Cannons successfully deliver 3 direct hits on the Imperial Corvette, the organic heat rounds burning through inorganic scientifically engineered shields. 

Meanwhile, on the opposite side of the massive dogfight engagement around the *Crimson Justice*, 24 fresh TIE Bombers are each launching 2 concussion missiles at Davian's Squadron. The ARC-170s carry Proton Torpedoes, which they fire and, as a result, some Torpedoes intercept a portion of the Concussion missiles… but not enough of them. The remainder of the concussion missiles find their marks, destroying all dozen Old Republic ships, but not before 7 of the TIE Bombers are gunned down by Davian’s pilots. 3 Concussion missiles stream after Davian’s Aethersprite. Davian Evades all three missiles, taking out one more TIE Bomber in response. Lexi can feel Davian’s presence in the Force now, his aura clearly corrupted by some dark alchemical disease. 16 TIE Bombers of the original dozen remain in the sky, after the exchange.

In another part of the massive dogfight swarming around the ISD, Master Xoda and his remaining 3 X-wings are ranged against a crimson Lambda Shuttle and its 7 winged mutant cohorts. 6 Proton Torpedoes flare out of the X-wings, rotating as they fly forth. 3 of the Torpedoes hit, killing 3 of the Flying Homunculi. 2 more Homunculi are shot down by red laser cannon bolts. In return, 2 X-wings perish to the claws and teeth and tails and tentacles of the flying mutations. At the end of the first deadly pass, only 1 X-wing remains alongside Xoda’s specialized miniature starfighter, while Vaz’ Forces have only 2 Homunculi left flapping alongside the Crimson Lambda Shuttle.

The second of the two Lambda shuttles and accompanying flock of 9 Homunculi attempt to dive onto the TIE Defenders engaged with the Raider. After a furious exchange of muscle, teeth, claws, limbs and green laser cannon bolts, all 9 of the Homunculi Perish, taking 2 TIE Defenders with them careening out of the sky.

Breaking through the red smoke, all of a sudden Xod's Dragon reappears, bearing the maniacally laughing alchemist atop its neck. The Dragon jumps onto Breggor's Corvette group, 3 of the 5 are able to swerve out of the way, thanks simply to Mandalorian Muscle Memory kicking in at the last second to save their lives. Unfortunately 2 of the Gauntlet Corvettes are ripped apart mid-air, as the Violet Nano-Mutated Dragon fights furiously with 3 bites in quick succession, 2 wing slashes, and 1 tail swipe. Breggor angles his helm to view the new threat, cursing obscenities.

Lexi’s small Coralskippers continue to do their jobs, coordinating activation of another set of 10 micro-black holes, eagerly slurping up the energy from the ISD’s failing single left-over Shield Generator. Despite the heavy assault of the Charybdis Dragon, Breggor knows he has to carry out his orders, synchronizing another launch of Proton Torpedoes together in concern with his 2 other Gauntlets remaining, launching 12 proton missiles in sync at the ISD. Blue detonations spark, reflecting off the Mandalorian’s helm visor. The Crimson Justice eats up the small barrage seemingly without umbrage, but by sheer luck that last Proton Torpedo, which hits it in the central Conning Tower, triggers a series of secondary fault line explosions that splits the ship in half down the middle. The Mighty ISD begins to crack apart and disintegrate as uncontrolled explosions overtake the grandiose imperial war machine.

Xod curses, unable to catch up to the agile Mandalorian Machines, he instead chooses to take out his frustrations on Xoda and his wingmate. Two claws catch the X-wings in their grasp, ripping it apart, while black teeth bite down and close over Xoda’s starfighter, swallowing it whole. Lexi winces, feeling Xoda’s soul extinguish in her telepathic consciousness.

The Core of Lexi’s Battle Group has coalesced into another formation getting ready to make another pass at Dark Wraith’s Raider Corvette, remaining Lambda shuttles and Homunculi, all of which had been badly depleted by previous separate dogfight engagements.

Lexi stands up on the bridge of the *Jedi Spirit*, her flagship. Throwing back her white cloak she points at the break in the clouds, as she narrows in on her target: the imperial Raider Corvette. ** “Spirit… ready Volcano Cannons, *Now Open Fire!*” ** 4 meaty thunks of glowing heat rounds splash into the ship containing Vaz and Dark Wraith. They turn to hug each other as blinding light engulfs them, followed by a flash heat and blast wave, evaporating them both. Vaz’ psychic scream can be heard in Lexi’s mind, its echo shattering the very reality of the Spirit World around her. Lexi snaps awake on the Garden Bench all of a sudden, breathing hard, sweat streaming down her temples. She takes deep inhalations of the fresh air around her, realizing that at last… finally… her Test is Over.

Looking down, she finds herself wearing a new set of white Jedi Robes, engraved with green vines and made of the finest high quality materials. She sees that her yellow lightsaber, constructed in the Spirit World, now hangs at her belt, as does a new pouch of 7 purple seeds. She waits a moment to catch her breath, understanding the weight of the feat she had just succeeded, letting it sink in.

*A long time ago, this True Jedi Robe belonged to one of the first Jedi Masters in the History of all Jee'dai. It will automatically resize itself in the Spirit World to take on the shape suitable to a new wearer. Inside the left chest pocket, is a window into a Pocket Force Dimension of the Jedi Master's own creation. This pocket requires Force focus and concentration while opening and closing, and while placing objects or taking objects out. It can store up to 100 kg of objects, the opening is about the size of a bowling ball. While in the Pocket dimension, objects and living things are placed into Temporal Stasis. Additionally, these robes permit the user to apply themselves with the Force down to a molecular level, modifying the very DNA of living things themselves, thanks to special enchantments interwoven into the wrist cuffs, that allow microscopic manipulation of matter by Force Projecting Nanotools, manifestations limited only by the user’s imagination. A Mentor is usually necessary to teach functional use of this Skill, as meddling with nano-scalpels randomly will usually not yield any fruitful results. The Robes grant an extra Force Point while worn (+1 FP). For the cost of a mental Force Point, they allow the user to Teleport up to 10m away, though clothing or objects that are not Force-attuned to the user like their Lightsaber or Jedi Robes cannot travel with the user, of course anything within the Extradimensional Pocket does travel successfully.*

*These robes also enhance the wearer's ability to call on and use the Force (+1 UTF). The wearer, if struck, can transfer the Force into the fabric of the Cloth to tank some of the damage incurred in combat, though this depletes the user's mental Force Point reserve. Additionally, the Robes carry within them the knowledge of the Jee'dai Master's Signature Force Power, the ability to Freeze Time for a target for up to 30 seconds (or between 1-5 rounds, depending on UTF DC 15/20/25/30/35), the volume of Temporal Stasis is also dependent upon the UTF check rolled (starting with the size of a bowling ball, each category upgrade costing -DC5 of the UTF result rolled: bowling ball, 1 sq, 4sq, 9sq, 16sq). While in Stasis, the target is invulnerable to outside harm, as objects attempting to strike them freeze in time instantly when coming into contact with the Stasis Field surrounding the target for the duration selected. Lastly, while worn this robe grants its user +1 to UTF to activate Temporal Stasis. The materials of this Robe were also very carefully selected in its construction. The cloth of this Robe shimmers and sheens with silvery thread, following the Spirit Force Points of its user, the silver threads woven into this robe enable its user to act much more magnetically and express themselves more fully around others, enhancing their speaking ability and force of personality. The Reactive Nanoweave fibers that make up the bulk of the cloth twist and squeeze the fabric of the robes in sync with the user's movements, linked to the Cerebellum of the wearer mystically through a spiritual link in the Force. This enhances dexterity, coordination, and consequently the ability to deliver force into an exact area of space, enhancing reflexes and fighting ability as well as the capacity to dodge incoming attacks.*

*Lastly, the Cloth inner layer is padded and so comfortable, as to hug the user and allow their skin to be blissfully encased in what feels like a cool soft pillow, even if struck by a vibroknife. This usually enables enhanced concentration and uninterrupted clear thinking, unless the damage is so severe it breaks the concentration threshold, which depends on Jedi skill and the amount of damage incurred. While wearing this robe, subconscious anxiety is partially suppressed allowing the wearer to reach higher levels of thought and imagination: this includes faster mental calculations, clearer forms of verbal expression, the ability to learn additional languages (+1 Language), learn additional skills (+1 Skill), and enhance creativity overall.*

Lexi takes in a deep calming breath, settling her racing heart. Reaching up to wipe away the sweat from her brow she finds her clothing had changed, much as had occured while she was in the Vision Quest.  Surpised she looks around partly suspecting the ordeal was not over. The garden was how she had left it and when her eyes fall on Wraith.

The Sith Companion she had encountered on Myrkyr sat across from her on the opposite bench, serenly sitting an looking her in the eyes. His expression was sad, serious, and somber. His levity had fled, replaced by a mature piercing gaze. His hair had turned white, and he was now wearing black Jedi Robes, interspersed with Golden Runes and Cyan stripes. Recognizing her, the corner of his mouth smiled. He nodded at her, bowing respectfully with his head before his smile spreads further.
**"Welcome back."**

Her own relief is palpable as she gazes over at him and she can't help but to return his smile. Like her, he had undergone a transformation, and she knows it would take them both some time to realize the full extent. Slowly she stands, stretching out muscles that had been confined for who knew how long. "**You did it,**" she starts as a congratulation, her smile widening as she closes the distance between them.

He stands up, but makes no move to embrace or hug her, despite it feeling as the right thing to do. He seemed hesitant, which seemed out of character for the brash Sith. What was he now, exactly? The piercing cyan gaze had remained, but his affect and palpable aura within the Force had changed, somehow. His gaze hardened as he lowered his chin to meet Lexi's gaze. **"You, also. I was worried you would never wake up... I should have known to have more faith in you than that. You had begun to turn ashen. I was beginning to think you would join the ranks of the... petrified drop-outs."** He points to the row of Jedi Statues, sitting at some of the other park benches.

Looking at the statues, her expression grows more somber. Wraith's own demeanor towards her too has changed, which she takes note of and causes her to pause and examine him more closely. "**Master Forus spoke truly when he said the trial would be difficult.**"

Wraith looked into the distance, eyes glazing over for a split second: **"Difficult... Yes, difficult... This place steals your soul you know, many Jedi arrive in this Garden, to dream a thousand dreams, and never leave. I can understand why so many spirits have made this their home... Doomed as it might be."** Gesturing, he points to the spreading corruption, which had fully eroded half of the entire Spirit Garden by now. Many of the Jedi Spirits were gone by now, having detached themselves from this place. Only a few remained gliding back and forth across the periphery of Lexi's Force Senses.

Lexi closes her eyes as the impressions of the souls she had encountered on her Spirit Quest as they had died suddenly come to the fore. A shiver comes to her unbidden in response. Did this place really steal their souls and now they were truly gone? Her fingers brush against the small bag of seeds Okhama had given her. She has the means to save this place, whether it was too late or not. Perhaps though, this place was better returning to the Force as all things must in time. "**Perhaps... it's time for it to return to the Force,**" she voices the thought and opens her eyes and looks up at the changed Wraith.
The Spirit of the hairless former Jedi Master had floated up to the duo, his expression aghast.

content: **"You're back... I don't believe it. No one has returned from the Spirit World for thousands of years..."**

content: Turning her attention to Forus she gives him an even stare before he gaze goess to the statues of the others. "**Thousands of years because no one has come? Or the success rate is truly that low?**" Her gaze comes back to the master. "**How long were we gone?**"
**"I don't know... I didn't expect you to return... Let me think... A day perhaps?"**

content: "**A day?!**" Lexi looks aghast at Wraith before pulling out her part lightsaber, part super computer to see if it can confirm exactly how much time had passed.

Straightens, bending at the waist in a bow. **"My humblest apologies, to you both. I underestimated your abilities, it seems. Allow me to introduce myself again. I am Arch Sentinel Ken'ray Forus, at your service."**

content: Lexi gives the confirmation to Wraith regarding the elapsed time before turning her attention back to Forus. "**You said before you knew the Temple well and that when we passed the test we'd be able to get to the hanger bay... The Dark One has a two day head start on us. We can't delay any longer.**"

**"Yes of course... You need simply to Walk the Verdant Path--"** He gracefully gestures to the Stepstone Path exiting from the Spirit Garden, though this one didn't seem very verdant anymore, all its surrounding greenery having been wilted by the corruption. **--Or at least... That's what we used to call it... Along the way, the Temple will guide your path, taking you wherever your heart desires... even to other places, other times, other... worlds."**

userName: Ken'ray Forus, Jedi Master Force Ghost

content: Lexi looks at him in suprise. "**It's like the World Between Worlds?**"

**"Only the Wisest of our Order can activate such interdimensional powers of travel. For first time Walkers, it would be challenging to simply navigate around the Temple itself... much less manipulate the spectrum of temporal continuity itself. That reminds me... I can't help but think that those robes you wear, look familiar... Very familiar indeed... but I can't seem to put my finger on it. Forgive me, Young Jedi... It has been thousands of years since I walked the Mortal Plane. My life, to me, is but a distant memory at this point..."**

author.global_name: Ken'ray Forus, Jedi Master Force Ghost

userName: Ken'ray Forus, Jedi Master Force Ghost

content: The change in topic has Lexi looking at confusion down at the robes she awoke in. "**It was worn by someone before me?**" Seeing the leaf design on the sleeves she makes a mental leap. "**Another Ardena perhaps. One who followed the Verdant Path?**"

The Sentinel shrugs, **"Who knows? The wisdom of the ancients is infinitely old. The Jedi are a tradition not merely bound by blood ties, but by spiritual bloodlines that transcend mortal concepts of ancestry."**

author.username: Ken'ray Forus, Jedi Master Force Ghost
author.global_name: Ken'ray Forus, Jedi Master Force Ghost

userName: Ken'ray Forus, Jedi Master Force Ghost

content: "**Spiritual bloodlines? Bloodlines are generally described through genetics, though I know when it comes to the Force, just because a parent has an affinity to the Force doesn't preclude their children will as well.**" She pauses as she considers.

**"Of course... Mortal Science has convinced everyone that genetics is the key to inheriting Force Sensitivity. Except... when such scientific concepts are applied to the real world, all of a sudden the self-professed experts are unable to explain why Force Sensitivity is passed on or not passed on, to the next generation."**

author.username: Ken'ray Forus, Jedi Master Force Ghost
author.global_name: Ken'ray Forus, Jedi Master Force Ghost

userName: Ken'ray Forus, Jedi Master Force Ghost

content: Lexi gives a small shake of her head. "**There's a scientist who was able to isolate the gene and create clones who retained their Force Sensitivity or seemed to be so when they weren't before. He may not have figured out why inheritance isn't absolute, but he certainly figured out how to minipulate it.**"

**"Science is only one Way of Knowing. It is limited by its own rules and limitations. The Force is Limitless and has no such weakness, nor has it any need to be validated or explained by self-agrandized Mortal Tinkering, do not forget. So called Scientists, have only ever separated the Soul, or Spiritual Essence, of the Force, from the contents of their experiments, leading to unrestrained and uncontained fragments of the Dark Side to be left over in its stead."**

author.global_name: Ken'ray Forus, Jedi Master Force Ghost

userName: Ken'ray Forus, Jedi Master Force Ghost

content:
Lexi frowns and shakes her head. If such was the case, then Xod wouldn't have needed her to complete the creation of his perfect Sith Homunculus.  Her conversation with Xod and with the mutated Wraith was something she was still trying to process. 
She hesitates a moment as she tries to find the words for her next question. "**During the quest: was it really the Soul of the person? If they died there, are they gone here as well? And if they were already dead... How does it all work?**"  Her thoughts raged with questions regarding those she had met during the quest. Had it really been them or just some construct of her own memories and the Force. Xod, Kryze, and Vaz had already met their ends and while she held hope she suspected Davian and Xoda had died on Tython after she left. There was also the dozens of pilots who had served in the squadrons of her fleet during that final battle. She had gone on the quest believing it was her own life she was risking, but for others to have become involved for her sake; had she known she probably would't have agreed to participate.

**"Yes and no. What souls you encountered on your Spirit Quest, were no doubt familiar to you. In fact, their central core comes from their presence in the Force in the present. However, it also comes from their Soul's signature on the past and future as well. Additionally, they can alternatively be thought of as not the Souls of those known to you interacting with your own soul, but instead as Your Memories of Their Souls interacting with your soul. Thus, if killed inside the Spirit Quest, your memories of that Soul will die, yes. However, the individual in the real world will not be effected by this death, however you may lose all of the memories you've had of that person up until that point. A single-subject amnesia would most likely result."**

author.global_name: Ken'ray Forus, Jedi Master Force Ghost

userName: Ken'ray Forus, Jedi Master Force Ghost

content: **"Another theoretical outcome is a single-subject algesia, which would mean that bringing up their Soul to mind would cause immense pain in your psyche, due their loss suffered during the Spirit Quest."**

userName: Ken'ray Forus, Jedi Master Force Ghost

content: "**Well, that's something of a relief...**" Lexi says, mulling over his words. "**And the knowledge I learned... was that true or something my own memories was creating?**"

**"It was knowledge granted to you by the Force. It's not necessarily the truth, but it is a true representation of what the individuals within the Spirit world *would* say, were they actually present and speaking to you."**

content: Slowly Lexi nods. "**And these new garments of ours...**" She glances over at Wraith's new garments as well before turning back to Forus. "**You mentioned they once belonged to another. Will we have to master the Verdant Path to learn more about them or is there any information about them out there?**"

**"The Robes of a True Jedi are a special piece of Spiritual Faith Armour. It's knowledge is innately transferred directly into your mind, via a spiritual Force link. If you but direct your mind's eye towards your Robes, you can *feel* what the robes are capable of providing you. It is already built into their function."**

She gave me seeds.**" Pausing she gentlty pats the pouch at her belt. "**It may be too late, but I could plant one to see if maybe this place could be saved one day.**" She turns to look at Forus.
**"It might be a waste of a seed, as I understand it, there is some kind of Dark Side Corruption spreading as we speak."**

content: "**Master Gellendor has been guiding me in the Verdant Force.  I may be able to clear the Corruption if I were to find the source.**"

**"Once long ago, I guarded this Temple with My Life. However, now I am relegated to being a dusty relic spiritually attached to this Garden of Spirits. What goes on outside of this chamber is unknown to me. All I can say, that is that source lies elsewhere in this Temple, but I know now where."**

userName: Ken'ray Forus, Jedi Master Force Ghost

content: Wraith had been occupied with making the rounds about the Garden, returning to the two Jedi conversing. **"I tire of listening to Dead Jedi... What did this one call itself, Arch Sentinel? What a pompous title. No wonder you Jedi allowed yourself to become extinct. Here the once mighty Jedi are little more than Trapped Ghosts, clinging to the remnants of an Age of Jedi Long Past. It is well past time, that I released them from this place... Released them *all.*"** His fists had begun to glow a fiery glow, erupting in flames.

Lexi looks alarmed and hesitates only a moment before Interjecting herself between Wraith and his intended target. "**And what exactly will that do?!**" She looks at his flaming fist warily.  "**They're already starting to leave this place. There's no reason to force them to!**"
**"Merely to Cleanse this place of Spiritual Debris."**
Forus had squared off against the white-haired Sith, taking up a High Crane Stance, one foot on the ground. **"I sense much fury with you, Jedi. Have I done something to offend you?"**

**“The past day or so, we’ve been feeling the ground rumbling underneath the plants of this garden. Mini quakes. This whole Temple is built upon a massive Supervolcano. Therefore, this could mean that somehow, the Withering has undermined the supports of the Temple itself. The Volcanic Forces of Nature, are no longer held at bay by Okhama. Which means, that any day now, the Volcano could erupt and incinerate any left standing here. Use the Verdant Path to escape this fate, if this were to happen, as a means of retreat. However, one can only be transported among other Jedi Temples or Forests, when using the Temple’s Verdant Path Power. Beware, many have ventured on the path never to return again. It is a pathway too mystical to even pretend to understand. The best thing to do, is to simply Walk the Path and Trust that it will guide you where you need to go.”**
