content: **"How could such an order of Wise Wizards build their whole goddam house on an exploding Supervolcano?"**
**I guess their wisdom doesn't extend to Disaster Insurance Fees."**
**"Let's hurry and get the heck outa here, rather than stand around chit chatting undead ghosts... if we linger long enough, we'll join em."**

Lexi can't help but let out a huff of laughter as she turns away from the spot Forus had disappeared from and faces Wraith. "**Are we trying to get out... or proceeding to the central vent where the hanger and your counterpart is heading to?** She steps up beside him, lowering her voice so it doesn't travel but between them. "**I suppose at this point do we leave and try to shoot him down if he manages to make it before the volcano errupts? There's the possibility he's already gone.**"

**"Well... if that old bald guy was telling the truth... Then we just Believe Very Hard, tense our foreheads, and we should be able to squint really hard until we wind up where we need to be, right? How about we just really really wish, to get to the closest ship off this rock? Then we'll just let the temple decide where on the mountain we wind up? It's either that or... just Wish for our Path to walk us off this planet, but ideally... I kind of want to keep our own Freighter, in case we need to, I don't know, let's say hypothetically, fly somewhere in the future at some point down the line."**
A small rumble shakes the cavern, very briefly.

**"Look at it this way... Either the Dark One is already gone, and this place is just left crawling with more Undead Knights. Or... He's still stuck in the mountain, and will most likely be incinerated along with all his Mutant Friends in one glorious eruption of titanic proportions. Right?"**
**"Either way, there is really no reason to stick around."**

Lexi nods her agreement. "**None at all.**" Turning toward the pebblestone path she sets her foot upon it. "**Perhaps while we walk... we can share what we learned on our quests.**" She glances sideways at Wraith to gauge his expression.
**"You can share. I, prefer to keep that information private."**
He sets off down the path at a brisk pace.

She frowns at him, before she moves to keep pace with him. His pace however proves to be faster than is comfortable for her shorter legs. "**Something... happened...**" She manages to get in enough of a breath to get the rest out at once. **"That changed your mind about us?**" She couldn't help but wonder with the way he had been acting towards her. Distant. He had begun opening up to her, but after their experience he was keeping his distance.

**"Something happened alright, you could say that yeah... but I don't intend to let that stupid little charade back there, effect how I feel about anyone, much less you. I'd be a fool to let those Jedi cause me to second guess any aspect of myself or those around me."**

Lexi grabs his arm to beckon him to slow down his pace so she could more easily keep up. "**It would also be foolish to ignore what it was you learned... The Force showed you what it did for a reason... The reason isn't always clear, but it's there.**" She pauses to let the words sync in. "**You don't have to to tell me what happened, but don't do yourself a disservice from thinking what the visions weren't meaningful. Even if it does mean how you feel about me has changed.**"

Stopping he turns around with a bored look, **"Are you gonna be like this the whole way? I faced down my fears, and I learned a warm, touching lesson about myself. Happy now? I don't want you to be second guessing every time we're apart that our relationship has changed. We just need to focus on the task ahead while we're 'working' and then, later, when we're together... maybe we can relax, and I don't know, do something that involves just us."** Tearing his arm away, he once again turns and starts walking down the Verdant Path.

Lexi quickly decides it isn't worth pushing and brings her own expression to one of neutrality. "**Fine then. Priorities. Getting out of here before the whole place explodes beneath our feet.**" Her focus turns to the Path in which they're heading. Wraith had suggested thinking about heading to the closest ship so she focuses on that desire. Whether it takes them to the hanger bay or back to where the Antioch waited for them would be for the Force to decide.

Walking along the Verdant Path, the duo feels tremors and distant rumbling on occasion. Small rivulets of pebbles dribble from the ceiling. After walking 20 minutes, they come out of the Path into a wider forest path, from which the Placid Catacombs Cupula can be seen ahead in the break through the trees. The animals in the vicinity are restless, or spooked, and are running around the underbrush in haste.
Adjacent to the Cupula, the Antioch sits parked with its engines glowing blue.

Lifts the comlink to open a channel. **"N-Zee, respond. This is Lt. Kai, open the landing ramp and be ready to launch as soon as we're back on board with the Captain. Password? We don't have time for this, damn it. I'm going to retool you into a Toilet Droid you stupid walking tin can."**

Lexi pulls out her own commlink to confirm the password as they step out of the catacombs. "**NZ, have sensors detected any other ships taking off from the vicinit?**" She asks as they make a rush towards the ship.
**"Negative, Captain. Thank you for confirming the pass code. I am prepping the ship for launch."**
Side by side with Wraith, she nearly runs to the boarding ramp and only pauses as they reach the top. She turns to get a final look in of the temple before hitting the controls to close the ramp.
**"Captain we are detecting a freighter-size ship signature taking off from the temple. On screen now."**
The whole temple is rumbling and shaking, as birds everywhere have been rustled up and animals are making noises in the jungle, leaves shaking.
In the distance, a jet of magma erupts shooting its red superheated contents into the sky above the jungle canopy.

After a few more magma jets join the first one, a massive red wave of magma coalesces and begins to sweep across the jungle, incinerating every living thing in its path. The magma forms a tsunami wave and is heading the direction of the Antioch's landing site.
A group of Death Knights mobs out of the Cupula, running towards the Antioch from the opposite direction. Like a horde of zombies, they heedlessly run headlong branishing a cadre of red lightsabers.
"**NZ! Take off! Now!**"Lexi calls into the intercom. She draws her lightsaber just in case the undead Jedi get too close.

As the boarding ramp closes and the Antioch takes off from the Temple, Lexi can see the final glimpse of the zombie horde running towards her. The ramp shutting just in time, a single yellow heat impression is left on the bulkhead door of the boarding ramp, dimming to black, as its heat dissipates, and Lexi feels the ship shuddering violently. N-Zee takes off on the Antioch's repulsor lifts and turns it to angle away at the stars. Ahead of them, a distant Old Republic Shuttle is already climbing for orbit. Behind them, a red wave of magma crests and splashes onto a horde of Death Knights, dissolving them while drowning them in superheated lava. A single hand, skeletal and grasping at the sky, sinks slowly into the lava lake. As the focus on it dims, behind it we can see the Antioch's asymptotic ascent at the clear blue sky. The skeletal hand closes into a fist, but the Antioch has already escaped its reach, and the final Death Knight sinks slowly to be consumed by bubbling magma.
The Supervolcano explodes, and the entire Temple turns into a beautiful fireworks display of Titanic Natural Fury. It's contents are incinerated and engulfed in roiling magma.

Once the ramp closes, Lexi rushes to the bridge and witnesses the final moments of the temple as the volcano explodes as she takes her place in the pilot's seat. Intent on not letting the shuttle get away, she aims the Antioch on an intercept course.
"**NZ, can you boost the engines. We can't let that shuttle escape.**"

Arriving on the bridge, Lexi can see the Shuttle ahead of them has already reached orbit. It makes the jump for hyperspace, disappearing into pseudomotion. **"Captain, I'm sorry but it has fled. I did intercept its destination coordinates, however."**
**"The Coordinates match the known location of Planet Weyland."**

"**Weyland?!**" She looks over at Wraith, concern and confusion evident in her expression. There were too many possible reasons for his darker half to be going to the planet in which the clone vats were located. None of them were good. "**Seems we may need to up the time table in dealing with Weyland.**"

Wraith gets a serious look across his face. **"The way I see it, we could either follow him there directly, after sending a signal to Thrawn to meet us there. Or, we could rejoin Thrawn's armada first, and come up with a coordinated attack plan for assaulting Weyland, while the Dark One wreaks havoc and hopefully leaves us an opening to infiltrate Reaper Central, the Emperor's Storehouse Itself."**

"**How much serum do you think he has left?**" she wonders. "**We could get there and have to contend with Reapers turned Humunculi.**" Finally she turns to look at Wraith. "**What do *you* think the end goal is? To create more homunuli? He must realize by now that Xod put in the killswitch. Could he be looking for a counter?**"
**"No, none of that just feels right... There must some other, unknown goal, the Dark One is pursuing."**

Lexi pauses for a moment as her memory of the vision quest comes to the fore. "**I confronted him in the vision quest. He told me he would destroy the Emporer Reborn and Jade. Could he be going after the clone?**"
**"Whatever he said to you on your Vision Quest, you'd be the one to know his intentions better than I."**
Lexi nods, contemplating. "**NZ, cooridinate with the Chimera to rendeavous. Let's handle this on a united front with Thrawn.**"
**"Acknowledged, Captain. I have calculated a hyperspace vector for the Armada's Current Location. It is gathering on the outskirts of the Obroa-Skai System. ETA 31 hours and 51 minutes."**
"**Can you calculate from there how long it would take to get to Weyland?**"
**"Yes, approximately 12 hours 30 minutes Captain."**
"**And from here? How long before that other ship arrives?**"
Its yellow eyes blink rapidly, **"Calculations complete: Approximately 28 hours 45 minutes, Captain."**
**"Assuming that Fortitute-class Shuttle has a factory standard hyperdrive."**
"**He'd have almost a day to enact whatever plan he has before we get there. What's the probability it doesn't?**"
**"Unable to compute, Captain."**

"**Thanks, NZ.**" Lexi stares out into the void of space as she weighs the pros and cons to going to Weyland following the Dark One or meeting with the Chimera first as planned. "**NZ, did Kryze send out any transmissions while we were on Dantooine?**"
"**Before he died, of course, but after Vaz and Wraith staged their coup.**"
**No outgoing transmissions on our records, during that time, Captain. Perhaps he was waiting for a complete interrogation of the, uh, suspect... before sending his report to Thrawn?"**
**"None of that, N-Zee. Just remember that he fought valiantly striving to safeguard the Captain's life."**
**"And gave his life courageously?"***
**"And gave his life courageously."***

"**Let's proceed with the plan to meet up with the Armada. Whatever the Dark One has planned... the armada may be enough to counter it.**" with a glance back at Wraith she gives a small shrug. "**Besides... we gave Thrawn our word**"

**Computing navicourse now, Captain. Computation successful. Inputting data into the Hyperdrive engines. Data upload successful. Hyperspace on your mark, Captain."** The hyper-thruster handle hisses, unlocking on the ceiling of the bridge.
**"Right. Let's get off this hippie planet and back to civilization."**

Lexi stands and heads to the central platform and the lowered handle, pulling it down closer to her. She gives Wraith a grim smile at his eagerness. "**To civilization...**" she comments dryly as she activates the hyperdrive. She watches as the stars distend and with it comes the fluttering in her belly; would she ever get used to the shift into hyperspace? Once the ship stabilizes she disengages from the control and lets it reset into the bridge of the cieling.

"**Is there anything else to report, NZ?**"
**"Negative, Captain. What are your orders for the duration of the jump?"**
"**Were you able to pull up any information regarding Weyland?**"
**"Information on screen now, Captain."**

content: "**Another wild world, Wraith. With your preference for society I expected you to have been born on more industrialized world.**" Her tone is light as she glances over the dossier NZ has presented.
Wraith rolls his eyes, **"Oh its very industrialized. One could even say that I came off an assembly line, and that wouldn't be too far from the truth."**

"**Suppose it depends on how one looks at it,**" she looks up at NZ and gives him a nod. "**We need to make best speed to the armada and we need to be in top form for going to Wayland. Are there any outstanding maintenance or repairs we need to address while we have the time?**"
**"All have been completed while the Antioch was grounded, Captain."**
**"Hyperdrive reports output at 101% of normal capacity, shall I increase its thrust output more Captain?"**
"**Yes, as long is it won't be causing any additional issues down the line.**"
**"Small chance of blowout Captain, but present nonetheless."**
"**We'll have to take the risk then... Keep an eye on the hyperdrive and mitigate as needed.**"
**"New settings uploaded. Hyperdrive now reports 111% function."**
"**Keeping with current function... what's our new ETA?**"
**"28 Hours 40 Minutes."**

Lexi frowns and then nods. Every little bit would count. "**Thank you, NZ. Please keep us apprised of any developments regarding the ship and our journey. It's imperitive we make it there without delay.**" With her orders given she glances at Wraith and after giving him a small nod she heads into the living area of the ship. It was time to unwind from the events of the last couple of days, starting with a shower and then some food.
After a screen wipe, the heroes have changed out of wargear and are eating a well deserved rehydrated meal.

Lexi sits at the bar in her tank top and loose pants, and languidly sips the hot cup of tea in her hands. She's remained quiet, unusually contemplative and she focuses on her food and tea with a single mindedness.
Having changed into a black bathrobe, he strolls into the Galley and has a seat next to Lexi, fingers nimbly placing items onto his plate.
**"Looks yummy. Killing like infinity Death Knights is hard work -- Mmmm!"** He bites into a juicy reheated pirogi.
"**Death Knights, Mutants and Vision Quests... Quite a full day,**" she agrees, finishing off her own plate of food and putting it into the washer. She resumes sitting and sipping her tea.

**"I think that calls for some downtime."** Turning to round on the Jedi, he looks her up and down, **"I trust you're ready to go to bed after this? I think we've both earned some well deserved... relaxtion time."**

Her thoughts, which had been wandering elsewhere, come into sharp focus as he turns directly to her. For a long a moment, Lexi just blinks as she brings her thoughts back to her surroundings. "**Relaxation time...**" she repeats and then a blush comes unbidden at the lecherous looks he is giving as he looks her over. "**And what... exactly,  did you have in mind?**" she dares to ask as she meets his gaze.
**"Oh only a little light sex, was all. I hope you didn't fall in love with someone else while you were on your Vision Quest?"**
He grins out of one side of his mouth.

Lexi blinks at him and then cocks her head at him. "**Love?**" she gives a short laugh and decides to tease him. "**Well... for all you know, I managed to live an entire lifetime where I never left Tython...Where I returned to my village after my Knighting and lived my life.**"

**"Oh yeah? Sounds kinda boring, if you ask me. I prefer my Vision Quests action-packed and with lots of violence and dark, futuristic elements."** He munches on some tea biscuits, narrowing his eyes thoughtfully.

Lexi looks away at her tea as the memory of the first Vision she had been given comes to the fore. The helplessness as she watched her unkown cousin burn in the turbolaser fire. "**It was nice...**" she starts haltingly. "**Until a Star Destroyer arrived and bombarded the surface.**"
**"That's what I'm talkin bout-- Oh wait, uh... Must have been frightening for you to witness. What do you think it means?"**

Lexi gives a small shrug. "**It was a view of what might have been had I not gone through the portal; a different path where I gave my place to Thrantir. Also... a realization of a fear I already had. Did the Emporer send not only you and your squad to stop us at the Temple, but also a ship to annhilate any trace of the Jedi on Tython.**"
**"I don't recall any plans to send an ISD back in time, no."**
**"Wait I just realized... What's become of your little community on Tython? Should we change course and see if they're still around?"**

Lexi lets her half ful mug clatter onto the counter. "**No,**" she says firmly. "**It's not worth the risk of what the Dark One can achieve.**" She didn't want to admit to Wraith that she was still afraid of what she might learn and she wasn't sure she could face the survivors knowing she had not yet accomplished the task for which their sarcifice had been made.

**"Leave it to my Darker Half to go and save the world for us both, he'll probably tear all the Reapers to shreds and turn them into mindless mutants. Let's hope they all just eat each other and we can arrive to nuke them all from orbit with Thrawn's help."**
"**Thrawn won't if it means destroying the cloning facility.**"
**"Oh yeah, well then how bout this instead... Why don't we just broadcast them a message saying we'll turbolaser them all into a fine paste if they don't hand over their cloning cylinders."**
**"I hope they say no, and then we can blast em anyway."**

"**May not be needed...**" She allows the change in their conversation to send her thoughts in another direction. "**Unless he finds a way to pass his mutation to the others or finds the correct compound... they'd be dead in 24 hours.**"
**"The killswitch, yeah I found out about that too, but Xod mentioned to me that Charybdis had evolved past that limitation already."**
"**He told me it was due to the regenerative nature of your bloodline,**" Lexi says slowly, contemplating. The Force, Forus had said would reveal the truth as if it was the person giving it.
"**Only your children or those with your regenerative bloodline would be able to overcome it.**"

** "Maybe the killswitch was killing a critical organ, like the heart or something, and my regeneration ability prevented the Homunculus from dying by regenerating the organ back to a healthy state..."**
"**He told me it was a compound... Hexalite, I think. Once it leaves their system it causes their bodies to shut down.**"

**"Hexalite is an element, likely a part of a coenzyme. Without this element, the coenzyme would be rendered nonfunctional. The regenerative powers likely manufacture some kind of analogous enzyme, and in this way, replace the function of the Hexalite coenzyme. Still... Without Hexalite their bodies would be weakened. Perhaps that is their weakness... Could he be going to the storehouse to look for the Emperor's Storehouse in search of a database of Hexalite Storage sites?"**
"**You would know more than I what is at the storehouse... if that's something that would be there, then it's not outside the realm of possibilities.**"

"**It's why I don't want to delay any longer than we need to. There are too many variables. Ultimately, if what I learned in the Quest is true, he'll move to kill the Emperor Reborn and Jade. He won't be able to do that if he's not at full strenght.**"
**"The Storehouse might itself hold Hexalite. Who knows how powerful that... Thing, could become, if it sucks up an industrial tank full of the stuff."**
Lexi shakes her head. "**The more we talk... the more I think I made a mistake not going right to Wayland.**"
**"Who knows what would have happenned then... Vaz could have wreaked a lot more havoc if she had a whole Imperial Base to play with."**

"**Vaz doesn't concern me... she's dead.**" Suddenly she stops, the Reaper's retort to Lexi having said as much ringing in her ears. Her gaze meets Wraith's and he can see a trace of fear in her cyan eyes. "**She is dead, right?**"

**"Pretty sure I sliced her down the middle. Hard to forget. Then again, we Reapers are hard to kill."** Grins, **"Don't worry so much, as I recall we saw her disintegrate into ashes. Pretty hard to come back from that."**

Her fingers trace the scar Vaz lightsaber had left on her chest. "**I was barely concious. I don't remember much.**" She lets out her breath in a sigh and then dumps the remnants of her forgotten tea into the reclaimation unit. 

"**We've gotten a bit off target if you wanted to engage in recreation,**" she says, lightening her tone and trying to change the course of their thoughts to other things. Tuning to look him full on, she raises an eyebrow. "**To bed... my love?**" She adds the last, half in jest and partly to see how he'd react.

**"That's the spirit. I mean... Yeah, sure I guess... We better get to bed right away, I don't want to be tossing and turning all night thinking of our next set of enemies. Maybe you can help to put me on my back?"**

Lexi smiles broadens. "**You know.. I have noticed: the nightmares you had that first night we shared a bed and you watched over me... you haven't seem to have had any since we've been together. **" Slowly she leans in towards him, close enough that her hand comes to rest on his knee beneath the bath robe. "**Been having sweet dreams instead?**"
**"I can confirm, that my dreams are definitely of higher quality, than before. Although, if I were to tell you their contents, I'm pretty sure you'll think considerably less of me."**
"**Are they base in nature?**" Lexi asks with an amused curiosity, and her hand moves in sync with her words as she gently tickles the underside of his knee.
**"Rather than weave words together... why not just show you?"**

Lexi's eyebrows raise at his declaration, when only moments before he was sure she would think less of him for such dreams. "**Yes... Why don't you?**" She challenges softly her hand returning to the top of his knee and gives it a small squeeze.

**"Gladly, but first... as is customary, we should engage in a game of words. The loser must please the winner and be their servant the rest of our hyperspace trip. That even includes service in bed, I might add. Are you in for a game?"**
"**And who or what will decide the winner?**" she asks raising an eyebrow at him.
**"The winner shall be decided by unanimous agreement. The loser must be gracios in their loss, don't you think."**
Lexi inclines her head in agreement. "**Is this going to be another game where neither of us can agree on philosophical vs theological? **"
**"I hope not, maybe lets stick to an area of common ground this time."**
Leaning back on the barstool, she removes her hand from his knee as she looks up at him. "**Alright... what's your game?**"
**"I haven't thought that far ahead. Nowadays, I mostly go where the Force wills, and make up the rest."** His eyes spark purple for a brief second as he says this.

She looks at him with surprise with this statement, but is surpised even more so by the shift in his eyes. "**Your eyes turned purple just now. You know... I don't think I've ever seen your eyes not touched by the Force.**"
**"Oh yeah? It's a bit new to me as well, I have to admit..."**
**"I don't want to get into it, but I've had a rather interesting Spirit Quest, and I'm not even sure what all of it really means..."** He trails off in thought.

**One thing I know for sure. There are waaaay too many things happenning to us, in too short a time. Like, improbably too many things. Large important events, once-in-a-lifetime sights, occurances, coindicindences, deaths, etc. This cannot be explained by mere logic. The living Force must be somehow directing our actions, there is truth in the idea that you are the Chosen One."**
"**Or... together... we're just good at attracting attention,**" she says dismissively as she always does when the topic of the Chosen One comes up.
**"Apparently, even the Chosen One can be killed, if what you saw of the Prophecy Stones on Tython was true, the previous Chosen One - even the Force Itself made an error in judgement it seems."**
**"How does it feel to be Chosen by Destiny Itself? We were both Chosen, it would seem, by Forces outside of our control, to be placed in this 'attractive situation' as you put it."**

**"Worst of all, what does it say about the Force if its prophecied Champion, whose name was Skywalker I believe, turns out to be a False Hope? If the Force can be Wrong, then... it could be wrong about other things also."**

"**The Force is beholden to the Wills of sentients... it's not predestination.**" She pauses when he asks her how it felt and she frowns. "**Maybe it wasn't wrong, but because of decisions and events, like using Time Travel, it changed the natural course.**"
